## Eaton Fire Victims Assisted by Pasadena Congregation

![Two women hold relief packages amongst the crowd at the event.](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/01/25125948/XL-PIO-CFH-PASADENA-002-JS-001.jpg)Victims of the Eaton Fire in Los Angeles County are provided relief goods by the Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ) in Pasadena.

*Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ) Holds Care For Humanity in Los Angeles County**January 24, 2025 (PASADENA, California)*— While the devastating Eaton Fire continues to burn, affecting over 100,000 residents in the area, the [Iglesia Ni Cristo (INC or Church Of Christ)](https://iglesianicristo.net)of Los Angeles County made sure the community of Altadena and its surroundings received immediate disaster relief through their Care for Humanity event held at the place of worship of the local congregation of Pasadena on January 18, 2025.

The residents, some of whom lost their homes in the fire, were invited to the event to receive a free hot meal, cases of water to bring with them to their evacuation locations, and a bag of relief goods (containing food, masks, and other items to assist in recovery). They also received  inspiring and uplifting words of encouragement from INC ministers at the site.

“This is very important, to help them in their time of need…They have lost their homes, everything. We can give them things they need, though it’s limited. But [it is very important] also to let them feel that they are loved, and that we care for them,” stated Brother Artemio Pilon, Jr., District Minister of [Los Angeles County](https://directory.iglesianicristo.net/districts/los-angeles-county)where 14 congregations of the INC can be found along with dozens more congregations in nearby counties.

The assistance provided by the [Care for Humanity](https://incmedia.org/community-receive-help-through-care-for-humanity-event-in-calgary-canada/)event, in collaboration with the Felix Y. Manalo Foundation, aims to address immediate concerns of the residents affected by the wildfires like the members of the De Ocampo family who were among those who attended the event.  They were very thankful that the Care for Humanity provided cases of drinking water for the affected residents.

“The water, it’s so limited right now. We could really use that…My parents are so thankful for the water, and also the food that was in the bags as well,” said Harvey de Ocampo. His family discovered that their house was one of only three in their neighborhood to have survived the fire.

The FYM Foundation conducts Care For Humanity events across North America, providing direct aid to families and charitable organizations in communities affected by disaster or long-term poverty. Globally, the Foundation responds with disaster relief aid while combating chronic poverty with work placement opportunities on its eco-farms, among other socio-economic lifting endeavors.

*With congregations in 165 countries and territories, the Church Of Christ continues to remain a pillar in communities around the world. For more about the Church and its activities, please visit www.iglesianicristo.net.*

Brother Artemio Pilon, Jr, Minister of the Gospel of the Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ), welcomes victims of the Eaton Fire to the Care for Humanity event that was held on January 18, 2025. Among the supplies provided to the victims of the Eaton Fire by the Care for Humanity event hosted by the Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ), were cases of drinking water for them to bring back to their evacuation locations. Members of the Altadena community, victims of the Eaton Fire, were invited to receive relief packages during the Care for Humanity event hosted by the Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ) on January 18, 2025 in Pasadena, California. Members of the Altadena community, victims of the Eaton Fire, were invited to receive relief packages during the Care for Humanity event hosted by the Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ) on January 18, 2025 in Pasadena, California. Members of the Altadena community, victims of the Eaton Fire, listen to the short program prepared for them during the Care for Humanity event hosted by the Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ) on January 18, 2025 in Pasadena, California. Eaton Fire victims attending the Care for Humanity event comfort each other in facing the tragedy of losing their homes, after hearing inspiring biblical encouragement from ministers of the Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ). Members of the Altadena community who were among the victims of the Eaton Fire attend the Care for Humanity event hosted by the Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ) in Pasadena, California on January 18, 2025. Community members of Altadena and Pasadena gather together for encouragement and relief efforts during the Care for Humanity event hosted by the Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ) on January 18, 2025 in Pasadena, California.

### La Clave Para Conocer Realmente a Dios

¿Cómo puedo conocer a Dios? ¿Cómo puede la fe ayudarme a conocer a Dios y dirigirme a la salvación? Aprenda con nosotros estudiando las palabras de Dios escritas en la Biblia.

### ¿Quién Puede Predicar la Palabra de Dios?

¿Necesito a los mensajeros de Dios para explicarme Sus enseñanzas incluso si yo mismo leo la Biblia? Aprenda a quién se revela el misterio de la Biblia.

### La Verdadera Naturaleza de Cristo

¿Es Jesucristo dios u hombre? Conozca el único Dios verdadero presentado por el Señor Jesucristo.

## ¿Está Cerca El Fin Del Mundo? Señales Bíblicas Que Usted No Puede Ignorar

Aprenda qué pasará en el Día del Juicio según las profecías bíblicas y por qué su decisión hoy puede cambiar su destino, en este episodio de El Mensaje. Pause Play 00:00 32:24 Unmute Mute Disable captions Enable captions Settings Captions Disabled Quality undefined Speed Normal Go back to previous menu Go back to previous menu Go back to previous menu 0.5× 0.75× Normal 1.25× 1.5× 1.75× 2× 4× PIP Exit fullscreen Enter fullscreen Play Pause Play 00:00 32:24 Unmute Mute Disable captions Enable captions Settings Captions Disabled Quality undefined Speed Normal Go back to previous menu Go back to previous menu Go back to previous menu 0.5× 0.75× Normal 1.25× 1.5× 1.75× 2× 4× PIP Exit fullscreen Enter fullscreen Play Play Higher ResolutionPlay Lower Resolution[Download Video](#elementor-action%3Aaction%3Dpopup%3Aopen%26settings%3DeyJpZCI6IjQ5MDMxIiwidG9nZ2xlIjpmYWxzZX0%3D)[Ask a Question](/contact/)Click to expand transcript

El Mensaje – ¿Está cerca el fin del mundo? Señales bíblicas que usted no puede ignorar.

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**Dondequiera que miren, hay inquietud: desastres climáticos, caos político y profundas divisiones entre las personas. Es difícil ignorar que algo grande se avecina, algo que podría cambiarlo todo.

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**Nuestras decisiones y acciones ahora darán forma al futuro. La pregunta es, ¿actuaremos a tiempo para evitar lo peor, o estamos encaminados hacia un momento en el que tendremos que enfrentar las consecuencias de lo que hemos hecho? Mantenganse conectados para descubrirlo en El Mensaje.

INTRO

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**Hola queridos amigos y bienvenidos a este episodio de El Mensaje. Soy el Hermano Christian Tantay. Seguimos extendiendo nuestra humilde invitación para que permanezcan con nosotros durante todo este programa. Un saludo a nuestros queridos espectadores y también queremos darles las gracias de todo corazón por dedicar su tiempo para  acompañarnos en este programa.

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**Tal vez la mayoría de ustedes ya han escuchado el término «Día del Juicio. Cuando se menciona ese término, ¿qué es lo que suele venir a la mente? Muchos creen que ese día es el día en que los malvados serán castigados. De ahí que la gente desee recibir la salvación en ese día. También hay quienes se muestran escépticos y se preguntan: ¿Sucederá realmente el Día del Juicio? ¿Qué prueba que el Día del Juicio tendrá lugar? Esto es lo que vamos a estudiar en este programa, El Mensaje.

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**En la Biblia, el Día del Juicio ya fue predicho en el pasado. Leamos **Tadeo 1:14-15 La Biblia de las Américas:**

- **De estos también profetizó Enoc, en la séptima generación desde Adán, diciendo: He aquí, el Señor vino con muchos millares de sus santos, para ejecutar juicio sobre todos, y para condenar a todos los impíos de todas sus obras de impiedad, que han hecho impíamente, y de todas las cosas ofensivas que pecadores impíos dijeron contra Él.**

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**Fíjense, queridos amigos, en lo que nos dice la Biblia: que «el Señor» vendrá «para ejecutar juicio sobre todos». Incluso desde  antes, durante el tiempo de los patriarcas, el Día del Juicio ya había sido proclamado.

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**¿Quién es el Señor que vendrá en el Día del Juicio? No es otro que el Señor Jesucristo. Es nada menos que el Señor Jesucristo Esta fue la declaración del Señor Jesús en **Juan 14:1-3 La Biblia de las Américas:**

- **No se turbe vuestro corazón; creed en Dios, creed también en mí. En la casa de mi Padre hay muchas moradas; si no fuera así, os lo hubiera dicho; porque voy a preparar un lugar para vosotros. Y si me voy y preparo un lugar para vosotros, vendré otra vez y os tomaré conmigo; para que donde yo estoy, allí estéis también vosotros.**

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**¿Cuál es la razón por la que Dios estableció o designó el Día del Juicio que será llevado a cabo o ejecutado por el Señor Jesucristo? Podemos encontrar la respuesta aquí en **Sofonías 1:14-18 La Biblia de las Américas:**

- **14 Cercano está el gran día del Señor, cercano y muy próximo. El clamor del día del Señor es amargo; allí gritará el guerrero. 15 Día de ira aquel día, día de congoja y de angustia, día de destrucción y desolación, día de tinieblas y lobreguez, día nublado y de densa oscuridad, 16  día de trompeta y grito de guerra contra las ciudades fortificadas y contra los torreones de las esquinas. 17  Traeré angustia sobre los hombres, y andarán como ciegos, porque han pecado contra el Señor; su sangre será derramada como polvo, y su carne como estiércol. 18  Ni su plata ni su oro podrán librarlos en el día de la ira del Señor, cuando por el fuego de su celo toda la tierra sea consumida; porque Él hará una destrucción total y terrible de todos los habitantes de la tierra.**

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**De modo que Dios fijó o designó el Día del Juicio a causa de los pecados del hombre. Tengan en cuenta lo  que la Biblia dice: «Cercano está el gran día del SEÑOR  … porque han pecado contra el Señor».

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**Tal vez otros digan : «No estoy haciendo nada malo. Me esfuerzo por hacer el bien todo el tiempo. Así que no hay nada que temer cuando llegue ese día». Sin embargo, esa idea es falsa y peligrosa. Porque según la Biblia, todas las personas han pecado, como podemos leer aquí en **Romanos 5:12 La Biblia de las Américas:**

- **Por tanto, como el pecado entró en el mundo por un hombre, y por el pecado la muerte, así la muerte pasó a todos los hombres, por cuanto todos pecaron-.**

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**Observen que la Biblia dijo que todos han pecado. El único hombre que no cometió pecado es el Señor Jesucristo. Debido a que todos han pecado, todos están sujetos a ser juzgados en el Día del Juicio.

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**Por lo tanto, queridos amigos, el Día del Juicio es algo que todas las personas deben tomar en serio. Ese es el día en que Dios ejecutará Su Juicio a través del Señor Jesucristo contra aquellos que han pecado.

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**Sin embargo, algunos pueden ser escépticos si el Día del Juicio sucederá o llegará. ¿Cuál es la prueba de que el Día del Juicio seguramente se llevará a cabo?

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**La gente debe entender que la voluntad de Dios que está escrita en la Biblia es la verdad. Eso se afirma aquí en **Juan 17:17 La Biblia de las Américas:**

- **Santifícalos en la verdad; tu palabra es verdad.**

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**¿Qué significa que la palabra de Dios es la verdad? La palabra de Dios se cumplirá o sucederá como se afirma en **Isaías 46:11 La Biblia de las Américas:**

- **Yo llamo del oriente un ave de rapiña, y de tierra lejana al hombre de mi propósito. En verdad he hablado, ciertamente haré que suceda; lo he planeado, así lo haré.**

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**Así que, lo que Dios declaró ciertamente sucederá. ¿Qué declaró Dios, incluso hace mucho tiempo, durante el tiempo de los patriarcas? El Día del Juicio. ¿Cuál es una verdad escrita en la Biblia que prueba que lo que Dios dijo se cumplirá y una de ellas es el Día del Juicio? Aquí en **Hebreos 9:27 La Biblia de las Américas:**

- **Y así como está decretado que los hombres mueran una sola vez, y después de esto, el juicio,**

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**¿Verdad  que, pase lo que pase, el hombre experimentará la muerte? Es inevitable porque fue Dios Quien la designó. Así como la muerte que Dios  estableció se ha cumplido, también se cumplirá el Día del Juicio. Por consiguiente, el Día del Juicio seguramente sucederá o tendrá lugar. En verdad, la gente debe tomarlo en serio y no ser escéptica al respecto. ¿Cómo podemos saber que el Día del Juicio está cerca?

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**Lo que leeremos es una ocasión durante el tiempo en que el Señor Jesucristo estaba todavía aquí en la tierra. Leamos **Mateo 24:3, 33. Reina Valera 2015:**

- **3 Estando él sentado en el monte de los Olivos, sus discípulos se acercaron a él aparte y le dijeron: —Dinos, ¿cuándo sucederán estas cosas? ¿Y qué señal habrá de tu venida y del fin del mundo? 33 Así también ustedes, cuando vean todas estas cosas, sepan que está cerca, a las puertas.**

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**En esta ocasión, los Apóstoles preguntaron cuándo llegará el Día del Juicio. Ellos preguntaron: “¿Y qué señal habrá de tu venida y del fin del mundo?” ¿Qué dijo Cristo? Él respondió: “Cuando vean todas estas cosas, sepan que está cerca, a las puertas.”

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**Así que Cristo dio señales que indicarán que el Día del Juicio o el fin del mundo está cerca. ¿Cuáles son estas señales? Continuemos leyendo en **Mateo 24:6-8, La Biblia de las Américas:**

- **6 Y habréis de oír de guerras y rumores de guerras. ¡Cuidado! No os alarméis, porque es necesario que todo esto suceda; pero todavía no es el fin. 7 Porque se levantará nación contra nación, y reino contra reino, y en diferentes lugares habrá hambre y terremotos. 8 Pero todo esto es solo el comienzo de dolores.**

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**¿Cuáles son las señales dadas por Cristo que indican  que el Día del Juicio está cerca? Cristo dijo: “Y habréis de oir de guerras y rumores de guerras.” Debemos entender que esto no es  una guerra ordinaria porque esta guerra sería rumoreada  por todo el mundo.

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**¿Cuál es el cumplimiento de esta profecía? La Primera Guerra Mundial, estalló el 27 de julio de 1914. Los historiadores confirman que esta guerra comenzó en esa fecha. Esto es lo que se menciona en **The Story of the Great War, Vol. 2, p. 923 (291):**

- “La primera gran campaña en los campos de batalla del sureste de la Gran Guerra comenzó el 27 de julio de 1914, cuando las tropas austríacas emprendieron su primera invasión de Serbia. Cruzaron la frontera serbia en Mitrovitza, a unas cincuenta millas al noroeste de Belgrado, empujando a los serbios ante ellos.”

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**¿Quién más atestigua que la guerra profetizada en Mateo 24:6 fue la que estalló en 1914? Los traductores de “ **The Last Days Bible**“, publicada en 1999 y traducida por estudiosos del griego bíblico, explican en la nota al pie de **Mateo 24:6-8 (p. 54)**que la guerra mencionada en la profecía se cumplió con el estallido de la Primera Guerra Mundial en 1914.

- Jesús ahora les dice a Sus discípulos cómo podrían discernir cuando el mundo estaba realmente entrando en el comienzo de los dolores de parto que provocarían el renacimiento de Israel y, en última instancia, el establecimiento del Reino de Dios en la tierra. El acercamiento del final de la era se vería varios fenómenos en la tierra que ocurrirían al mismo tiempo. Esos fenómenos, serían: 1. Muchas naciones que se levantan contra muchas otras naciones 2. Reinos que se levantan contra reinos 3. Hambruna 4. Epidemias de enfermedades 5. Terremotos en varios lugares.

- Guerras entre naciones, o incluso entre reinos, es y ha sido un acontecimiento común. La historia ha registrado hambrunas en varios momentos. Ha habido tiempos de grandes epidemias de enfermedades. Ha habido terremotos en el pasado, pero cada vez más en la actualidad. Sin embargo, Jesús dice a Sus discípulos que esperen un momento específico en el que todos estos fenómenos serían evidentes al mismo tiempo. El primero de tal tiempo en la historia del mundo ocurrió durante los años de la Primera Guerra Mundial (1914-1918) e inmediatamente después.

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**De manera que, el cumplimiento de estas señales que Cristo dio prueba a algo. Así como estas señales se han cumplido, el Día del Juicio también se cumplirá. Entonces, en nuestro tiempo, el Día del Juicio está verdaderamente cerca.

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**¿Y qué hay de la pregunta de los Apóstoles cuando le preguntaron a Cristo cuándo llegará el Día del Juicio? ¿Qué respondió Cristo? Esta fue la respuesta de Cristo, escrita en **Mateo 24:36 La Biblia de las Américas:**

- **Pero de aquel día y hora nadie sabe, ni siquiera los ángeles del cielo, ni el Hijo, sino solo el Padre.**

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**Observen, queridos amigos, que solo el Padre sabe cuándo tendrá lugar el Día del Juicio. Ni el Señor Jesús ni siquiera los ángeles del cielo saben cuándo llegará ese día. Solo el único Dios verdadero, el Padre, sabe  cuando  ocurrirá el Día del Juicio.

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**Dado que nadie lo sabe, excepto el Padre, ¿cuál es la instrucción del Señor Jesucristo con respecto al Día del Juicio? Esto es lo que Cristo dijo en **Mateo 24:44 La Biblia de las Américas:**

- **Por eso, también vosotros estad preparados, porque a la hora que no pensáis vendrá el Hijo del Hombre.**

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**Ya que nadie sabe cuándo tendrá lugar el Día del Juicio—solo el Padre lo sabe—la instrucción de Cristo con respecto al Día del Juicio es: “estad  preparados.” ¿Por qué? “Porque a la hora que no pensáis vendrá el Hijo del Hombre.”

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**Por lo tanto, sin ninguna duda, el Día del Juicio llegará. ¿Qué deben hacer las personas? Deben tomar esto en serio, obedeciendo la instrucción del Señor Jesucristo, el Salvador, a quien Dios ha designado para ejecutar Su juicio en ese día decisivo—debemos estar preparados.

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**Pero, ¿cómo puede uno estar seguro de que está listo o preparado para encontrarse con el Señor Jesucristo en el Día del Juicio? ¿Verdad que todos han pecado y que todos están destinados a experimentar la ira de Dios en ese día? ¿Cómo puede uno ser salvo de la ira de Dios en el Día del Juicio? Responderemos a estas preguntas después de una breve pausa, por favor, quédense con nosotros.

PAUSA

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**Bienvenidos de nuevo, queridos amigos, siguen viendo “El Mensaje”.  Anteriormente pudimos probar que el Día del Juicio ciertamente llegará. Dios declaró que Él ejecutará el juicio a través del Señor Jesucristo. Lo que Dios dice se cumple. Así como la muerte, que Dios declaró, se ha cumplido, el Día del Juicio ciertamente  llegará. Así como se han cumplido las señales que Cristo dio indicando que el fin está cerca, el Día del Juicio, verdaderamente, se acerca rápidamente. Por eso , las personas deben tomar esto en serio, cumpliendo la instrucción de Cristo—estar preparados.

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**¿Cómo puede uno estar seguro de que está listo o preparado para encontrarse con el Señor Jesucristo en el Día del Juicio? ¿Verdad que todos han pecado y que todos están destinados a experimentar la ira de Dios en ese día? ¿Cómo puede uno ser salvo de la ira de Dios en el Día del Juicio? Leamos **Hebreos 9:27-28 La Biblia de las Américas:**

- **27 Y así como está decretado[a] que los hombres mueran una sola vez, y después de esto, el juicio, 28 así también Cristo, habiendo sido ofrecido una vez para llevar los pecados de muchos, aparecerá por segunda vez, sin relación con el pecado, para salvación de los que ansiosamente le esperan.**

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**¿Cómo será uno salvo de la ira de Dios en el Día del Juicio? Según lo que hemos leído: “Así también Cristo, habiendo sido ofrecido para llevar los pecados de muchos,” o de aquellos a quienes Él salvará.

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**¿Por qué Cristo se ofreció a sí mismo para llevar los pecados de aquellos a quienes salvará? La Biblia dice: “Aparecerá por segunda vez, sin relación con el pecado, para salvación de los que ansiosamente le esperan..”

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**¿Qué significa esto—cuando Cristo murió por aquellos a quienes salvará? Aquí en **Romanos 5:8-9 La Biblia de las Américas:**

- **8 Pero Dios demuestra su amor para con nosotros, en que siendo aún pecadores, Cristo murió por nosotros. 9 Entonces mucho más, habiendo sido ahora justificados por su sangre, seremos salvos de la ira de Dios por medio de Él.**

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**Según la Biblia: “Pero Dios demuestra Su amor para con  nosotros.” ¿Cómo manifestó o demostró Dios Su amor? El versículo dice: “En que siendo aún pecadores, Cristo murió por nosotros.”

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**Entonces, ¿de qué están seguros aquellos que se beneficiaron de la muerte de Cristo? La Biblia dice: “ Entonces mucho más, habiendo sido ahora justificados por su sangre, seremos salvos de la ira de Dios por medio de Él.”

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**De modo que, aunque Dios ha designado el Día del Juicio para castigar a los pecadores, por Su amor y misericordia, Éldio a las personas la oportunidad de ser salvas de Su ira. ¿Cómo? A través de la muerte de Cristo para redimir a aquellos a quienes salvará, para que sus pecados sean perdonados y no sean condenados  en el Día del Juicio.

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**¿Puede alguien simplemente afirmar que está entre aquellos que fueron redimidos? ¿Quiénes fueron los que realmente se beneficiaron de la muerte de Cristo?

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**Uno no puede simplemente afirmar que está entre los redimidos. La Biblia dice claramente quiénes se beneficiaron de la muerte de Cristo. Leamos **Hechos 20:28, Traducción Lamsa:**

- **Tengan cuidado de ustedes mismos y de todo el rebaño sobre el cual el Espíritu Santo les ha puesto como supervisores, para apacentar la iglesia de Cristo la cual él compró con su sangre.**

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**La Biblia es clara  al identificar a aquellos a quienes Cristo redimió. La Biblia dice: “la iglesia De Cristo, la cual él compró  con su sangre.”

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**Entonces, ¿de qué están seguros los miembros de la Iglesia De Cristo? El perdón de los pecados y la salvación de la ira de Dios en el Día del Juicio. Así pues, aquellos que han cumplido verdaderamente con la instrucción de Cristo de estar listos o preparados para el Día del Juicio son aquellos que entraron  en  la Iglesia De Cristo.

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**¿Qué más prueba que la Iglesia De Cristo será salvada? Esto es lo que se dice en **Daniel 12:1 La Biblia de las Américas:**

- **“En aquel tiempo se levantará Miguel, el gran príncipe que vela sobre los hijos de tu pueblo. Será un tiempo de angustia cual nunca hubo desde que existen las naciones hasta entonces; y en ese tiempo tu pueblo será librado, todos los que se encuentren inscritos en el libro.”**

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**La Biblia afirma que aquellos que serán librados o salvados son aquellos cuyos nombres están escritos en el libro, el cual es el libro de la vida en el cielo. Algunos podrían argumentar que afiliarse a una iglesia no tiene nada que ver con la salvación. ¿Es esa afirmación cierta?

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**No, porque en Hechos 20:28, Traducción Lamsa, está claro que la Iglesia De Cristo fue redimida. Cristo murió por ella, por lo que la Iglesia es importante. Para  demostrar esto, la membresía en la Iglesia es necesaria para que el nombre de una persona esté escrito en el libro de la vida en el cielo.

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**¿Cuál es la prueba de eso? Leamos **Hebreos 12:23 Versión Biblia Libre:**

- **Han venido a la iglesia de los primogénitos cuyos nombres están escritos en el cielo; a Dios, el juez de todos, y donde están las personas buenas, cuyas vidas están completas.**

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**Como se dice aquí, el versículo menciona: “a la iglesia de los primogénitos cuyos nombres están escritos en el cielo.” Entonces, los nombres de aquellos que están en la Iglesia están escritos en el cielo. Sin embargo, ¿se salvará una persona solo por inscribirse en cualquier iglesia?

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**No. Una persona debe asegurarse de que la iglesia a la que pertenece sea la verdadera. Entonces, ¿cuál es esa verdadera Iglesia donde los nombres de sus miembros están registrados en el cielo?

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**Leamos **Hebreos 12:23**, pero esta vez leeremos desde el versículo 22 en **The Remedy New Testament:**

- **22 Pero han venido a la realidad (a la que los símbolos solo apuntan), al Monte Sion, la Nueva Jerusalén, la ciudad celestial del Dios vivo, y ustedes son parte de esa ciudad. Ustedes se han unido a millones y millones de ángeles que se regocijan en la presencia de Dios,****23****en el santuario celestial, la verdadera iglesia de Cristo, cuyas identidades están guardadas en el cielo. Han venido a Dios, la fuente y el estándar de justicia para todas las personas; a las individualidades archivadas de seres humanos justos perfectamente restaurados a la semejanza de Cristo en el carácter;**

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**La verdadera Iglesia donde los nombres de sus miembros están registrados en el cielo es la Iglesia De Cristo. Esta es la verdadera Iglesia que recibirá la salvación en el Día del Juicio, el cual ciertamente llegará y está muy cerca.

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**Y según el versículo que han leído, la ciudad celestial está reservada para los miembros de la Iglesia De Cristo. Ellos son los que Cristo les dio una promesa en el versículo que hemos leído anteriormente en **Juan 14:1-3**, donde Cristo declaró esta verdad: “ En la casa de mi Padre hay muchas moradas; … voy a preparar un lugar para vosotros. …, vendré otra vez y os tomaré conmigo; para que donde yo estoy, allí estéis también vosotros.”

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**Por lo tanto, uno debe asegurarse de ser miembro de la Iglesia De Cristo en ese día bendito. Que ustedes, queridos amigos, tomen estos pasos necesarios para ser salvos en el Día del Juicio.

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**También continuamos extendiendo nuestra invitación para que sigan examinando las enseñanzas que sostenemos en la Iglesia De Cristo. Les animamos a asistir a nuestros servicios de adoración y a los estudios bíblicos que se realizan en nuestras congregaciones locales cercanas a ustedes. Pueden visitar nuestros sitios web oficiales: [iglesianicristo.net](http://iglesianicristo.net), [incmedia.org/es](http://incmedia.org/es)y [pasugo.com.ph](http://pasugo.com.ph). Además, suscríbanse a nuestro canal de YouTube: “ [youtube.com/@incmediaespanol](http://youtube.com/@incmediaespanol).”

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**Y si tienen alguna pregunta, envíennos un correo electrónico a info@incmedia.orgy podremos ayudarles a conectarse con un ministro del Evangelio. También pueden visitar el canal oficial de YouTube de INC Media Español para aprender más.

**Hermano Christian Tantay:**En nombre de todos nosotros aquí en El Mensaje, nos gustaría agradecerles por unirse a nosotros hoy en nuestro estudio. Soy el hermano Christian Tantay. Hasta la próxima.

## Descubra Cómo Puede Guiarle La Biblia

[HACER UNA PREGUNTA](/contactanos/)

### PASO 1: RELLENE EL FORMULARIO

RRegístrese en la parte de abajo. Le enviaremos una respuesta y un horario que se adapte a usted.

### PASO 2: ASISTA A UN” MEET AND GREET”

Responderemos a sus preguntas mediante videoconferencia y compartiremos lo que podrá aprender en la Iglesia De Cristo. Y si usted tiene preguntas adicionales, podemos programar más sesiones.

### PASO 3: ESTUDIE A SU PROPIO RITMO

Cuando usted esté listo, inscríbase para los estudios Bíblicos. Nos adaptaremos a su ritmo para encontrar un horario que se adapte a su disponibilidad y ubicación.

## Regístrese para aprender más sobre los Estudios Bíblicos

Todos los campos del formulario marcados con * son obligatorios.

¿Qué quiere aprender? Seleccione uno… ¿Quién es el Dios verdadero? ¿Quién es Jesucristo? ¿Cómo puede la Biblia darme un propósito? ¿Por qué creer en la Biblia? Religión y si existe una verdadera Iglesia? ¿Cómo es una vida con Dios? Enseñanzas fundamentales enseñadas en la Iglesia de Cristo Otra Cuéntenos más sobre lo que quiere aprender. PROGRAMAR MI "MEET AND GREET"

## VER VIDEO: TODO SOBRE LOS ESTUDIOS BÍBLICOS

### ¿Por qué estudiar la Biblia con nosotros?

La Biblia contiene las enseñanzas de Dios. En ella usted puede encontrar la guía para la vida y lo más importante, las instrucciones para la salvación. Entonces, cuando se registre para asistir a estudios Bíblicos en la Iglesia De Cristo, obtendrá una comprensión de las palabras de Dios y cómo vivir una vida significativa de acuerdo con la Biblia.

### Los estudios Bíblicos son diferentes en la Iglesia De Cristo.

- Los estudios Bíblicos se realizan en un formato de pregunta y respuesta.
- Las respuestas vienen directamente de la Biblia.
- No hay opinión, especulaciones ni narraciones aleatorias.

### ¿Qué podrá aprender durante un estudio Bíblico?

- ¿Acaso Dios y Jesús son lo mismo?
- ¿Qué sucede después de la muerte?
- ¿Están las palabras de Dios escritas en la Biblia?
- ¿Son todas las religiones iguales?
- ¿Cómo puedo orar por mi familia?
- ¿Necesito formar parte de una Iglesia?

## Is the Bible Relevant

to Your Life?

Discover how a book written thousands of years ago is the key to you finding your purpose and navigating life’s challenges today.

[Register to Attend](#register)

You’re constantly looking for answers. *“What is my purpose?” “How should I live my life?” “Who can I trust for answers?” “How do I make decisions when there are so many unknowns in life?”*

While there are countless books and experts you can turn to, discover why the Bible can give you the best answers to navigate the uncertainties of life and help you find your life’s true purpose.

### Register and Learn

Join us for a 2-Day Bible-based Webinar on Sunday, December 1 and Monday, December 2 and learn the answers to questions like:

- Why is the Bible still relevant today?
- Why can you use the Bible to guide your life and help you make decisions?
- How can the Bible help you navigate the uncertainties of today?

#### Sunday, December 1 – “Is the Bible Relevant to your life?”

**Time:**6pm CT

**Where:**Through Webex, so you can connect from home.

#### Monday, December 2- “How Can The Bible Help You Navigate Life’s Uncertainties?”

**Time:**7:30pm CT

Where: Through Webex, so you can connect from home.

*This is a two-part webinar series but if you can’t make both nights, we’ll send you a link to the reply.*

### Want to know if the Bible is a source you can trust?

Register below to save your seat. We’ll send the details directly to your email.

### What to Expect During the Two-Day Bible-based Webinar

A message from Brother Roland Andrade, District Supervising Minister, Manitoba, Canada

## About the Speakers

![](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/11/08173712/Brother-Ramil-300x300.jpg)

Ramil Macaspac II is a Minister of the Gospel of the Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ). Born and raised in the Philippines, he entered the Holy Ministry in 2007 and was assigned in different congregations in the Philippines and Canada (Saskatchewan, Manitoba, and Ontario)

Brother Ramil is happily married and a proud parent of three children. He enjoys staying active through basketball, which serves as both a hobby and a way to stay fit. In his downtime, he enjoys watching movies and documentaries, always eager to explore new stories and ideas.

![](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/11/08173714/Brother-Jason-300x300.jpg)

Jason Abrias has been a Ministerial worker in the Church Of Christ (Iglesia Ni Cristo) for 6 years. Formerly from the Greater Toronto Area, he was assigned to care for congregations of the Church Of Christ throughout the Manitoba and Saskatchewan region. He is currently overseeing the congregation of Weston.

Brother Jason is a lifelong fan of the Toronto Raptors, enjoys working out, and loves eating food made by his wife, Frances.

### What are Bible studies like in the Church Of Christ?

In the Church Of Christ, we follow a question-and-answer format in our Bible studies. There is no personal interpretation or random storytelling. **Just God’s words to give you meaning and purpose in your life.**

Attending these Bible study webinars will help you:

- Explore how the Bible’s words apply to your modern life
- Find answers to your questions about life’s purpose
- Connect with others seeking meaning and purpose
- Leave with hope and guidance to navigate life’s challenges

No matter your background, you deserve the best guide for your life. Discover how the Bible can be the source of truth and direction you’ve been searching for.

### What to expect during the Bible study webinar?

Your time is important to us. Each Bible study webinar will start promptly at the scheduled time and will last no more than 30-40 minutes.

We invite you to log in early and invite friends and family to tune in with you.

**We’re excited to meet you!**

## Experience Hymns in the Worship Service

Discover how hymns can uplift and comfort you inside the Church Of Christ worship services. Discover melodies that speak what your heart cannot say.
- [Baptism](https://incmedia.org/tag/baptism/), [Church Membership](https://incmedia.org/tag/church-membership/)

![](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/13132412/Modern-Music-Playlist-Facebook-Post-1024x576.jpg)[Ask a Question](/contact/)

What is your favourite song?

Everyone has one, or multiple. Chances are, you have a playlist of all your favourite songs. But— why is it your favourite song?

There is a saying: “When you’re happy, you listen to the tune. When you’re sad, you listen to the lyrics.”

There might be multiple reasons for this. You might like the tune, or it’s a ‘feel-good’ song. You can have the worst day in your life, but when that song plays wherever you are, it instantly lifts your mood by 1000%. I know for a fact a lot of my favourite songs are catchy, but sentimental old me, my favourite songs are tied to experience in my life. They’re part of my wall of highlights.

## The songs on my playlist

I always go with “Better Than This” by Kimberley Locke when I think about my recent family week trip with my parents, and I was even blasting it in my headphones during midnight on New Year’s Day. It has a really hopeful tone of a confident future. “Scenery” by V of BTS is a special one I reserve for my memories walking the streets of London years ago. And until now, that song transports me back. Then there is “I Will Always Choose You”, which is an INC Original song, because yes, the Church Of Christ does have their own original music. That song is a great reminder to always choose God, no matter the circumstance.

![](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/13133957/Film-Frame-Birthday-Collage-Instagram-Story-1.jpg)

My absolute favourite songs are my favourite songs because of the lyrics. These songs weren’t written for me, but that is the beauty of music and lyrics, isn’t it? Words and tunes crafted together in one way, but mean something different for each person. Songs like that might define who you are, or a song that represents everything you’re going through right now. Songs have a way of expressing for you what you can’t.

What are my absolute favourite songs, you might ask?
You won’t find them on any music streaming app.

They’re not even on my playlist.

They’re known by a different name: Hymns.

## The Worship Service Hymns

I am a choir member in the Church Of Christ. My duty (or role) in the worship service is to lead the singing of hymns together with my fellow choir members. If you haven’t been to a service in the Church Of Christ, you are missing out. But let me tell you what you’ll hear first the moment you walk in: you’ll hear the hymns.

![](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/13132353/choir-singing-us-1024x683.jpg)

The hymns of praises open our worship services. You’ll hold a hymnal book, turn to a hymn number, and the choir members will stand up and begin to sing it. Led by the organist, the choir will stand up to sing each hymn, and the worshippers can pick up a congregational hymnal book (or follow the lyrics projected onto the screen) and sing along.

## What Makes Church Hymns Unique

Like other songs, the worship service hymns are crafted with words and tunes that harmonize to create meaning. But **unlike**ordinary songs, they serve another purpose: they help you talk to and praise our Lord God and Jesus Christ. They’re also an integral part of the worship services in the Church Of Christ.

The hymns are pieces of music that are solemn, joyful, and meaningful. They aren’t what you might think they are—they aren’t made for “mega-church” concerts, crafted with loud bass and pop beats.

The hymns are crafted for the worship service. Each word is heavy with meaning, and each note is heavy with spirituality.

Each hymn is special, but they help us talk to our LORD God and our Lord Jesus Christ. For members of the Church Of Christ, the hymns not only help us to say what we can’t find the words for but also serve as reminders.

You see, the hymns understand us. We all live different lives, we all go through different things. But the hymns provide specific reminders always to worship God, no matter what; to follow His teachings; to love the brotherhood in the Church. And if we listen closely to them, each hymn reminds us how to be close to our LORD God.

But, let me tell you what *my favourite*part of the worship service hymns is:

**They help me feel****seen****.**

And I’m not the only one.

## The Effects of Hearing the Hymns

I spoke to a sister from my local congregation, Sister Lois. She is not a choir member, however like me, she loves the hymn-singing part of  the worship service. She says:

**“****The hymns express our innermost feelings, which may be sometimes hard to express in our spoken language. Most of all, they express our gratefulness to God and our Lord Jesus Christ, as well as the Church Administration”**

![](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/13132415/two-women-smiling-767x1024.jpg)

She told me about a difficult time she and her family went through. Her mum had been diagnosed with cancer, and it was challenging for Lois. She told me how her mum would request songs, as a way to cheer her up.

There was a hymn that Lois related to the most:

**“Oftentimes, I sing with [her mother] “As for me and my Family.” It really gave us strength and always reminded us to stay strong as a family and reminded us of our commitment to God that no matter what happens we will serve Him.”**

And when her mother passed away recently, it took Lois a while to get up on her feet. But I always see her, one of the first people to walk into the house of worship, waiting for the worship service to start. Lois is one of the strongest people I know and a Children’s Worship Service Teacher. But even people who inspire us, feel defeated at times too. I asked her how she overcame her sadness, how she handled her mother’s passing, needing to fly from the Philippines back to Melbourne to continue with her life here.

She told me about a time she was teaching for the Children’s Worship Service. The hymns began, and there was one hymn that resonated with her:

**“The time I taught again in the [Children’s Worship Service] after my mom passed away, the hymn where it says “even though we will be orphaned” was sung and it was really something I needed. No matter what happens, God will never leave us.”**

You would think that hymn was coincidentally sung on a day Lois needed it most. But I say it wasn’t a coincidence. That is the power, and beauty of God’s love, through the hymns. This is precisely how hymns work.

## The Blessed Moments of Hymns

In a [Blessed Moments](https://incmedia.org/inspired-to-fulfill-my-lifelong-duty/)episode, Sister Mirasol Anzunas Padilla from Tarlac, Philippines, expresses her gratitude to our Executive Minister Brother Eduardo V. Manalo, for officiating a special worship service for choir members which she was blessed to attend. Sister Mirasol has been a choir member for 69 years, calling this her “lifelong duty”.

![](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/13132419/woman-with-toga-1024x560.jpg)![](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/13132402/choir-singing-1024x555.jpg)

She talks about how serving as a choir member all these years is the reason for her family’s peace and prosperity, from herself, to her children and grandchildren. She draws inspiration from the teaching of God, led by the Church Administration, to keep going in her duty. She says:

**“[The messenger] reminded us to live a righteous life, be diligent, and wholeheartedly fulfill our duties…that we should hold on to what God can do in our lives.”**

Just like Lois’s story, the hymns remind us of messages, just like what Sister Mirasol beautifully puts it. And that is a reminder to always believe in, trust in, and rely on what God can do for us. We should continue always to please Him, and when we cannot find the words to say, the hymns speak the words our hearts could not find.

It is no wonder why she remained a choir member all these years. As a choir member myself, I find hymns to be my greatest companion. I am blessed to have my music scores and look through them any time, and my duty as a choir member is a blessing from God. It brings me closer to Him, and whenever I sing, I feel closest to Him – as if He was right next to me, and I, holding my hymnal book, sing to Him as I silently plead for Him to look at my heart for the things I want to cry out to Him, that the hymns can kindly do for me.

## Want to experience the power of the worship service hymns?

If you’ve found this blog, and you have never heard of the Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ), or heard anything about our worship services, I encourage you to attend one. It is one perspective to read this blog, but you’ll need to find out more yourself. Even to just sit, observe, and hear the hymns of praises for yourself.

## What to Expect in Your First Visit

[You can watch this video on what to expect in a worship service.](https://incmedia.org/what-to-expect-in-an-inc-worship-service/)You’ll see what I mean when I say the hymns have a way of helping the members of the Church Of Christ feel seen.

It’s one of the elements that makes our worship services special, and unique from the others. The heart of our worship services is our Lord God’s teachings, but we start with the hymns to help us feel God’s presence. It is there for us to sing praises to God, to thank Him for the miracles He brings into our lives, and to help us put into words the troubles we have so God can bless us with what we need.

And when you’re ready:

[Find a worship service near you>>](https://incmedia.org/find-a-congregation/)

The next time you attend a worship service, tell me what hymn helped you express how you feel inside. [You can find me on Instagram](https://www.instagram.com/_littlekezmoments/)**.**You never know; we might have the same favorite hymn.

### About the Author(S)

## Kez Gutierrez

Kez is a secondary teacher from Melbourne, Australia. As a daughter of a minister of the gospel in the Church Of Christ, Kez has lived in places including the Philippines, Hong Kong, Thailand, and now Australia. Today she’s a volunteer talent for INC Media, a choir member, and a video editor for INC Music. When she’s not talking about her faith, you can usually find her enjoying a hot chocky (that’s chocolate for everyone outside of Australia), having Korean BBQ, or at the rink practicing her ice skating moves.

## Frequently Asked Questions

### Share

[Ask a Question](/contact/)

### God & Jesus

Is Jesus God?

No. Jesus is not God, according to the Bible.

Watch the [full video for biblical evidence](https://incmedia.org/is-jesus-both-god-and-man/)and the reason many mistake Jesus for God.

Why is the trinity wrong?

The trinity is not a teaching from the Bible. Learn and understand biblical truths that prove the trinity is a man-made teaching.

[Watch the full video](https://incmedia.org/why-god-is-not-a-trinity/)for the biblical answer.

Is there more than one God?

No there’s not. Learn how the Bible teaches that the Father is the only true God.

Watch the [full video for the biblical answer.](https://incmedia.org/why-god-is-not-a-trinity/)

Who is the true God?

Learn how the Bible teaches that the Father is the only true God.

Watch the [full video for the biblical answer.](https://incmedia.org/the-true-god-is-not-three-persons-in-one/)

### The Bible

Why are there so many Bible translations, and which one does the INC use?

The INC references verses from different translations of the Bible in [order to clearly and faithfully convey the pure message of God](https://incmedia.org/why-use-a-different-bible-translation/)in a given language. The Bible has been translated into many versions in different languages, making it accessible to people from different cultures.

### Salvation & Judgement Day

When is the Day of Judgement?

No one (not even the Lord Jesus Christ) knows the day and hour of the end of the world, except for God Himself. However, there are biblical signs that indicate when the end of the world or Judgment Day is near. [Learn and understand how recent events are signs that the return of our Lord Jesus Christ](https://incmedia.org/our-beliefs/the-second-coming-of-christ-and-judgment-day/)or the end of the world is near.

Watch the full video for the biblical answer.

How can I be saved?

Learn God’s instructions and requirements written in the Bible in order to meet His conditions to enter heaven.

Watch the [full video for the biblical answer.](https://incmedia.org/what-is-salvation-why-do-we-need-it/)

### Church Distinctions

Why do you say the Church Of Christ is the one true Church? Won’t all churches be saved?

Not all churches will be saved because not all churches are true. The true Church is not just any church that uses the Bible, but rather the Church that is introduced by the Bible. [Learn what the Bible says about the only Church](https://incmedia.org/does-a-true-church-exist/)that will be saved and how to identify it.

Watch the full video for the biblical answer.

Who is Felix Y. Manalo? Why do you believe Brother Felix Y. Manalo is the last messenger of God?

Felix Y. Manalo is the Last Messenger of God in these last days which was prophesied in the Bible. This can also be identified through the fulfillment of the prophecies concerning his place of origin, the nature of the work entrusted to him, and the time period when he would be commissioned by God to carry out His work. Brother Felix Y. Manalo, who preached God’s pure message regarding the true Church Of Christ, is the fulfillment of those biblical prophecies.

Watch the [full video for the biblical answer](https://incmedia.org/what-we-believe-about-brother-felix-y-manalo/).

### Holidays & Prohibitions

Why is eating blood prohibited?

The Bible clearly and strictly prohibits the eating of blood by his ancient people and in the modern Christian era. This is because God created blood to give life, to also redeem man from sins and not for consumption.

[Watch the full biblical answer here.](https://incmedia.org/is-eating-blood-a-sin/)

Why don’t you observe the Sabbath?

The Sabbath is a command of God recorded in the Bible for a specific people during a specific period of time. The INC firmly believes in and follows the biblical teaching for God’s people living in the Christian era.

Watch the [full video for the biblical answer](https://incmedia.org/is-the-sabbath-still-relevant-today/).

Why are INC members not allowed to date or marry someone outside the Church?

The INC follows the biblical teaching of God for His people not to unite in marriage with un-believers as it would lead them away from Him and to destruction.

[Watch the full biblical answer here.](https://incmedia.org/interfaith-relationships-do-they-work/)

Why doesn’t your Church allow divorce?

The INC firmly believes in and follows the biblical teaching of God on the sanctity of marriage and the prohibition of divorce.

[Watch the full video for the biblical answer.](https://incmedia.org/what-does-the-bible-say-about-divorce/)

### Membership

How do I become a member?

The Lord Jesus Christ is the One who established the process of entering or becoming a member of His Church. A person must first be taught or preached the Gospel by those whom He authorized or sent to preach the gospel. Those who believe in the teachings must be baptized into the Church Of Christ.

The Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ) implements the process established by Christ. A person must follow these steps to become a member:

Learn the basic Bible teachings upheld in the Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ) through Bible Study lessons.
Undergo a probation period during which a person is able to prove that he truly believes through obedience to the teachings of the Bible.
Be baptized into the Church Of Christ.

If you are interested, [we can help you get started to become a member.](https://incmedia.org/become-a-member/)

## Fe, Sanación y Comunidad: Historia de una mujer Colombiana

Floriza, mujer colombiana y superviviente de una enfermedad, encontró fe, guía espiritual y comunidad en la Iglesia De Cristo. Un testimonio de fe a Dios.
- [Christian Living](https://incmedia.org/tag/christian-living/), [Christian Relationships](https://incmedia.org/tag/christian-relationships/)

Play Higher ResolutionPlay Lower Resolution[Download Video](#elementor-action%3Aaction%3Dpopup%3Aopen%26settings%3DeyJpZCI6IjQ5MDMxIiwidG9nZ2xlIjpmYWxzZX0%3D)[Ask a Question](/contact/)Click to expand transcript

**Fe, Sanación y Comunidad: Historia de una mujer Colombiana**

**Floriza:**Mi nombre es Floriza Enciso Mendez.

**Floriza:**Yo nací en Villeta. Nací en una [familia católica](https://incmedia.org/mi-camino-de-la-iglesia-catolica-a-la-iglesia-de-cristo/)– católica, no practicante. Y luego a los ocho años llegué a Bogotá y seguí mi vida en Bogotá. Llegué a un convento de monjas de las comunidades San Pedro Claver y ahí estuve hasta los 22 años. Luego salí ya a la vida civil, estudié diseño de modas en ropa normatizada y pues mi sueño era tener mi empresa.

**Floriza:**Pero al cabo de unos años me dio una hemorragia cerebrovascular. Salí de la clínica y otra vez aquí mi trabajo de. Y pues me repitió. Entonces me dio la segunda hemorragia y luego vuelta a la clínica y mi empresa iba ahí, pero a la tercera. El tercer episodio de la tercera hemorragia es así, medio muy duro y pues en la clínica seis meses perdí la voz, perdí el movimiento y la memoria, sino la perdí. Entonces, luego de esto ya llevaba un mes en la casa.

**Floriza:**Yo visitaba familias e íbamos a visitar barrios vulnerables, un grupo, íbamos y visitamos la familia. Las invitamos a hacer oración o que tuvieran una visita de un de un sacerdote, pues para que los guiara y yo también tenía grupo en la casa. Yo estando tan pequeña en la Católica porque yo tenía fue que me en yo me vinculé al grupo de visitar enfermos y la hemorragia me dio antes de yo empezaba esa misión y yo estando preparada y me esperaban allá.

**Floriza:**Yo me cambié y dije no, yo no vuelvo por allá. Yo trabajaba firme en la [iglesia Católica](https://incmedia.org/el-origen-de-la-iglesia-catolica/), ahí se criaron mis hijos y yo iba y miraba y no sentía el calor de Dios, porque es que sentir [el amor de Dios](https://incmedia.org/el-unico-dios-que-debemos-amar/), el calor de Dios en mi, eso no tiene que perjudicarme, eso tiene que darme ánimo a seguir buscando de Dios.

**Floriza:**Yo tengo pues muchas amigas, muchas conocidas y ella recibió una invitación de un hermano de la Iglesia de Cristo. ¿Ella como pues como me conoció trabajando para la gente, dijo, “Ay Flor, vaya a esta reunión que es de oración?” Y si yo fui yo y me encontré a la hermana triste y ya hablé con ella, y yo le dije cómo hago para seguirlos contactando? Entonces ella me presentó al hermano Juan Fisher.

**Floriza:**Es que no pudiera venir aquí. Hasta aquí me queda muy lejos. Es difícil porque para pagar carro diario, para ir y venir es complicado, le dije. Con toda esta dificultad puedo yo hacer el curso. Y ofrecí y ahí hice el curso. Pero el curso del hermano Juan iba a la casa. Yo siempre que hice preguntas siempre me daban una respuesta basada en la Biblia y  correcta porque si fue la Biblia fue inspirada por Dios y creo en Dios.

**Floriza:**Yo creo en lo que está escrito en aunque siempre se basa a y la respuesta no la espera, que es la del pueblo, sino que la de el. La respuesta correcta de la Biblia. Entonces eso es importante. La misión me enamoró de la Iglesia de Cristo porque me parecía una labor muy bonita. Fue la primer vez que escuché la Iglesia de Cristo y me quedé.

**Floriza:**Yo no sé en si se Dios me hizo quedar porque yo siempre le pedí a Él que me que me diera la luz de ver dónde me pertenecía y creo que nací para pertenecer a esta Iglesia. Yo acepté como en el, creo que fue en el 2019 y yo feliz de que entendí que llegara el día del bautizo, porque ya fue el día del bautizo y yo le pedía a Dios que me ayudara a caminar más, a facilitarme el caminar y ya salí de ahí y yo con como con otra perspectiva de la vida y que ya no yo, ya puede caminar más y sí fui facilitando mí.

**Floriza:**Hoy día como estoy que estoy mucho mejor. Yo he ido a visitar muchas iglesias, iglesia de donde usted llega y y si alguien conoció otra iglesia, se van a hablar mal de esa otra iglesia y eso a mí me parecía que no era bien. Y me gustó que [en la Iglesia De Cristo](https://incmedia.org/es/iglesia-de-cristo-conozcanos/)no escuché eso. Yo en ese punto sí pienso que cada época trae una necesidad. Y no uno esas dificultades como que las supera. Si me dieran respuestas negativas, yo sabía que yo iba a meterme a la a pertenecer a la Iglesia de Cristo. Y eso fue lo que hice por convicción propia, no porque me dieran consejos o porque otro me diera que decía aquí y eso fue tan importante para mí que aquí me tienen, ahí estoy, ahí sigo sin saber de que él me tiene aquí de pie.

**Floriza:**Haberme dado la licencia, de volver a caminar, de volver a hablar, eso es lo que me fortalece en mi Señor. Pues ahí sigo, en la ducha para poder aprender más y pues saber que conocer más de Dios. Cada día quiero conocer más de Él.

## Fighting Loneliness With Christian Community

Discover why loneliness is an epidemic, understand your feelings of isolation, and find healing through Christian community and meaningful connections.
- [Christian Living](https://incmedia.org/tag/christian-living/)

![](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/05142418/BANNER-LONELINESS-1024x576.jpg)[Ask a Question](/contact/)

If I could draw a picture of what loneliness looked like it would look a little bit like this post from incmedianews’ Instagram account.

But loneliness doesn’t always look like the image above.

In fact, the more I talked to people, loneliness **may**also look like this.

This ![](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/05145238/woman_talking_JSalazar_INC1953-1024x683.jpg)

Or this.

![](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/05142430/parents_and_kid_on_computer_Mike-1024x577.jpg)

Maybe even this.

![](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/05142438/truth_genz_MMercado-0104-1024x683.jpg)

**But what you see on the outside may not always reflect what’s happening on the inside. The smiles on the outside may not always reflect the emotions and feelings of loneliness someone feels on the inside.**

As you can see, loneliness isn’t just being alone. And it’s a lot more common than you may think.

## What is Loneliness?

You can feel lonely in a crowded room or even when you’re surrounded by family and friends. It’s that empty feeling when you feel disconnected from others, even if people are around. Loneliness is when you want more social connections or deeper relationships than you currently have.

Loneliness might feel like:

- Feeling left out at school, at work, or at home
- Wishing you were in a relationship or had friendships
- Feeling like no one understands you
- Wanting to talk to someone but not knowing who

Remember, everyone feels lonely sometimes. It’s a normal human emotion.

While it can be uncomfortable, loneliness can also push us to make positive changes in our lives. For some, it might lead to picking up a new hobby or reaching out to make new friends.

### When Loneliness Becomes the Problem

Maybe you’ve seen the headlines about the “loneliness” epidemic. But the numbers are shocking considering we live in a time where we can connect face-to-face with people at any time. But consider the numbers from recent research:

- 4 out of 10 people feel lonely at least once or twice a week
- 79% of young adults often feel lonelier than those [aged 66 and older.](https://newsroom.thecignagroup.com/loneliness-epidemic-persists-post-pandemic-look)
- Single people tend to feel lonely more than married people
- 65% of parents and guardians are lonely compared to 55% of non-parents

In a world where we can talk to anyone anytime, people still feel alone. But don’t worry—we’re going to talk about why this happens, why it’s healthy, and how to fight it when it becomes a problem.

## Why do people feel lonely?

Based on all the recent research, observations and studies, it’s clear that there’s not one reason to explain people’s loneliness.

- Relocation and loss of familiar support systems
- Heartbreak and heartache
- Major life transitions (new jobs, schools, new child)
- Difficulty forming deep connections in new environments
- Past traumas or emotional struggles
- The challenge of building a new social network from scratch

I reached out to a few people who shared their recent experiences with loneliness and what has helped them.

### Mia from Canada moved from a bustling metropolitan area to a small city.

Having grown up in a very diverse environment with weekly church activities, Mia found herself in unfamiliar territory. The stark contrast in her new surroundings led to unexpected challenges.

Mia reflected:

>
>
> “I definitely faced culture shock. I felt so alone. Even when I made a couple of friends at work, although they were kind, I truly felt like they could not understand me, my experiences, and my values. It’s such an isolating feeling when you can’t connect with people that have the same values as you.”
>
>

### Meet Felicia, who experienced heartbreak after a long-term relationship

Felicia’s story highlights how the end of a significant relationship can lead to feelings of loneliness. She describes the difficulty of adjusting to life after her relationship.

>
>
> "I couldn't stand the fact of being home alone, so I would go out on long drives and listen to sad Taylor Swift songs on purpose just to cry it out. I found myself going to the gym two or three times a day and even signed up to run 10k's. I know, crazy right?"
>

### There’s also Cham C., a young Christian from Mindanao who relocated to Manila.

Originally from Mindanao, a southern region in the Philippines, She had traveled to Manila to represent her district in the International Unity Games of the Church Of Christ and was encouraged by her uncle to relocate permanently. All at once she was in a new place, started a new job, and transferred to a new local congregation. The [suddenness of these changes](https://incmedia.org/overcoming-the-fear-of-change/)and the difficulty of leaving behind close friends contributed to her feelings of loneliness.

>
>
> “Longing to start a new life, living in a new city, meeting new people, and starting a new job, I thought it would be easy. I'm so lonely, I didn't expect some things to happen to the point that the loneliness made me regret my decision.”
>

### Dylan, a college student from California struggling to find belonging

While he seems like your typical college student, Dylan has often felt detached and conflicted, caught between different emotions while trying to figure out who he really is. His loneliness doesn’t come from being alone, but from feeling disconnected from himself and constantly overthinking.

>
>
> "I found myself lost between confidence by consequence... juxtaposed between sporadic and indefinite periods of languishing and being conflicted towards sustaining regular periods of peace."
>

### And finally, there’s Abigail F., a young mom from Canada

When she first became a mom, Abigail found herself teetering between the wonders of motherhood and feelings of loneliness. She shared:

>
>
> “When I think that I'm not doing the best or the right thing, especially on my firstborn based on others’ standards as a mother i.e. breastfeeding, not giving healthy foods always, etc, I feel I'm lacking as a mother.”
>
>

![](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/05142414/ABIGAIL-FAMILY-PHOTO.jpg)

## Loneliness can be tough. And in the moment, can feel impossible.

**Loneliness isn’t an impossible problem to solve.**

While each person has faced moments of loneliness they found solutions in the faith community they surrounded themselves with.

Here’s what is helping fight off those feelings of loneliness.

- **Focus on God.**Abigail found that doing work for God made her feel more happy and productive. She felt less lonely in the process because she focused not on herself but on God.

*Praying and performing my duty as a choir organist have always been my “therapy”. By performing my duties to God, I know I am needed – that I am doing something good; that I am doing something that is pleasing to Him. This world is full of insecurities.*

*But when I am performing my duty to Him, I feel His mercy and love – that I am not alone. It’s like God is enveloping me with His warm embrace and telling me that, “It’s ok – you are not alone. I will always guide you. I love and appreciate you..”*
- **Work on your current relationships and build new ones.**With more time on her hands, Mia took the opportunity to deepen her relationship with her family. She also used the time to open herself up to new relationships.

>
>
> I broadened my definition of friendship – they don’t necessarily have to be people my age with the exact same interests. I started getting close with the people in our Group Worship Service, whether they were children, parents, or grandparents. It started to feel like family.
>

![](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/05142425/MIA-WITH-YOUNG-PEOPLE-1024x682.jpg)

**3. Christian groups.**Cham joined the Church youth group for unmarried individuals over the age of 18 and took part in Church activities. This helped her [meet new people](https://incmedia.org/friendships-rewind/)who liked the same things she did.

>
>
> “Every Church activity with them secretly heals me. The loneliness I felt faded away because they never let me feel that I am lonely, and He never fails to heal me every time I pray to Him.”
>

![](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/05144506/IMG_20241004_101829-805x1024.jpg)![](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/05144523/IMG_20241004_104704-1024x768.jpg)![](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/05144518/IMG_20241004_104139-1024x768.jpg)

**4. Spend time with people in real life:**It’s hard to believe that people are lonely when you consider how many people they have on their “friends” list on social media. But the truth is connecting on these platforms doesn’t always translate to meaningful relationships.

Instead, connect (or reconnect) with those already in your life the same Felicia did.

>
>
> “Thankfully, I had friends and family who were more than willing to listen, see me cry, and keep me company. From brunch dates, facetime calls, and BTS/K-Pop Sundays, I was beyond grateful for the ones who took their time to spend time with me and make sure I was okay.”
>

**5. Be grateful for what you have:**Instead of feeling sad about what they didn’t have, our friends focused on the good things in their lives. This helped Dylan feel more [grateful for the meaningful relationships](https://incmedia.org/expressions-of-gratitude/)he has now and his deepening relationship with God.

>
>
> "Only knowing all that I know now, I could only then definitively understand the importance of being able to preserve the friendships I’ve come to appreciate, but more importantly thoroughly understanding my relationship with God."
>
>

## Advice from the Bible

I asked Brother Joe Velasquez, a minister of the gospel, what advice he would give someone who might be feeling the heaviness of loneliness.

He said: “ *Being far away from our family, friends, and loved ones can often lead to feelings of loneliness and isolation. And while there are actions we can take and interactions we can make to help us break out of emotional tailspins, we should above all rely on our Almighty God to ease the pain of feeling alone, like what was done by an early servant of God who prayed to Him.”*

He read me these verses from the Bible as an added reminder:

**Turn to me, LORD, and be merciful to me,****because I am lonely and weak.****Relieve me of my worries****and save me from all my troubles.**

**Psalm 25:16-17 Good News Bible**

![](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/05142443/VERSE_PSALMS2516-18-576x1024.jpg)

We can be certain that if ever we feel alone, we can call out to our Almighty God in our distress, and He will surely be there for us and not leave us alone, because we put our trust in Him.

## Finding Community at Church

Want to know something cool? All the people I told you about found awesome friends at Church! They joined activities, helped others, and suddenly, they weren’t so lonely anymore.

**Cham shares,****“The KADIWA Organization, my Church duties, and the friendship I made in the local congregation I transferred in also helped me a lot.***The loneliness I felt faded away because they never let me feel that I am lonely, and He never fails to heal me every time I pray to Him.”*

**After her breakup, Felicia found solace in her faith and community.**She shares, *“I remember telling one of my really close friends I wanted to strengthen my relationship with God. She encouraged me to join our Church’s concert production, which coincidentally was about finding peace.”*

Felicia also became more involved at Church, which helped her heal and find genuine happiness.

![](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/05145019/FELICIA-INCONCERT-768x1024.jpg)![](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/05145022/photo_2024-12-05_09-13-49-768x1024.jpg)

**Instead of just keeping to himself, Dylan looked for more opportunities to participate**in his local congregation, participating in events like the *Making Changes*episode screening and youth group activities These moments provided him with a sense of belonging and reinforced his faith. *“I had a confirming feeling: “that my prayers, my thoughts, and my ambitions were thoroughly guided, and moreover validated.”*

**Mia found a new way to be involved in the Church’s youth group.***“I was asked to become a District KADIWA Officer. I was finally able to make those deep connections I was searching for, with people that shared the same faith and same values. I needed something to hold onto, and it was given to me. Through this duty, I am confident to say that I’ve made some of my closest lifelong friends.”*

![](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/05142422/ka-edwil-and-group-1024x576.jpg)

### Loneliness looks different for everyone

I remember the time in my life when it seemed like everyone around me was married with kids. I remember attending a friend’s baby shower and realizing I was the only single person there. I felt like I was on the outside looking in. These moments can make you question your life choices or wonder if there’s something wrong with you.

But I’ve learned that everyone’s journey is different. Being single doesn’t mean being alone or incomplete. It’s an opportunity to focus on personal growth, pursue passions, and build meaningful relationships of all kinds.

### My community of creative Christians

I’m blessed to be surrounded by the people I work with every day at INC Media. As coworkers, they became my friends and support system. Our group reminds me of the Japanese concept of social support groups that last a lifetime, providing social, health, and spiritual support.

We celebrate successes, help each other during tough times, and share in our faith journey together. Loneliness doesn’t stand a chance when you’re part of a supportive community. Whether it’s through Church, work, or other groups, finding your own community can make all the difference.

### About the Author(S)

## Renezen Benedicto

Renezen Benedicto is that introvert you probably saw in the corner reading articles on her phone. Born in the Catholic church her parents were introduced to the Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ) in the late 1980s by her grandparents. She currently produces the program Blueprint and is a staff writer at INC Media.

## First Pastoral Visit of the year 2026

On January 9, 2026, the Executive Minister of the Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ), Brother Eduardo V. Manalo, led a worship service at the Local Congregation of Zabarte in the Ecclesiastical District of Quezon City.

The Executive Minister reminded the Church members that with each passing year bringing even greater difficulties than the last, they must remain steadfast in their faith and hold firmly to God’s teachings. In his homily, he highlighted God’s early servants as examples, showing that those who trusted in God were never forsaken. Play Higher ResolutionPlay Lower Resolution[Ask a Question](/contact/)

## Former Protestant Chapel in Texas Acquired by INC

The Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ) has acquired a new chapel located in Sugar Land, Texas — a property formerly owned by the Grace Christian Center.
- [Church News](https://incmedia.org/tag/church-news/)

Play Higher ResolutionPlay Lower Resolution[Download Video](#elementor-action%3Aaction%3Dpopup%3Aopen%26settings%3DeyJpZCI6IjQ5MDMxIiwidG9nZ2xlIjpmYWxzZX0%3D)[Ask a Question](/contact/)Click to expand transcript

*[Show Open]*

**Michael Robinson:**Various religions are on the decline. A Pew Research report states that and this year alone, 15,000 churches could close in the United States. But in Texas, the growth of the true faith has resulted in the need for [more worship buildings,](https://incmedia.org/the-number-of-houses-of-worship-in-the-church-of-christ-has-increased-once-again/)the most recent of which is a chapel in Sugar Land, Texas. Let’s go to Myrtle Alegado for the story.

**Myrtle Alegado:**A significant moment for the Church Of Christ in Texas with a newly acquired worship building in the city of Alvin. Purchased recently from Grace Christian Church of Alvin, Texas. This chapel sits on five acres of land and currently accommodates 250 has a baptismal pool along with several office spaces. Approximately 30 miles from Houston, Alvin is a city known for its agricultural roots and oil was discovered in this area in the 1930s. With the [Church Of Christ](https://incmedia.org/seekers-find-answers-in-the-church-of-christ/)having a presence in the Houston area for 25 years, more and more people in the surrounding cities have also discovered the true faith. As membership has grown here, so too has the need to find a fitting and permanent place of worship for members from Sugar Land.

**Salvador Biazon:**We did our [devotional prayer.](https://incmedia.org/how-do-i-know-god-is-listening-to-my-prayers/)We drive all around the neighborhood almost every week just to find property. And I think one day, I just found this property, and right away, I messaged our resident minister. We contacted the realtor. That’s the start of the negotiation process for acquiring this property.

**Myrtle Alegado:**And the members here were overjoyed to reach this momentous milestone, marking not only a time of celebration, but also new opportunities for continued growth, both as a congregation and in faith.

**Edward Pingol:**And finally, we were able to find this place, and today we were able to close the deal. And finally, we have this house of worship at this very moment.

**Brother Ricky Bravo:**When the brethren heard about the decision of the [Church Administration](https://incmedia.org/our-beliefs/on-the-church-administration/)that they approved the purchase of the property. The brethren were so glad and so excited.

**Brother Noel Caritativo:**Today, the Church was able to finalize the purchase. And as we can see the brethren in Sugar Land are so happy that they now have their own permanent house of worship.

**Myrtle Alegado:**[Members](https://incmedia.org/why-so-serious-about-church-membership/)here wait in eager anticipation for the renovations of this worship building to begin as they will be able to continue on in true service to God once this chapel is dedicated to Him.

**Brother Noel Caritativo:**We would like to thank our Almighty God and the Church Administration, especially our beloved Executive Minister, brother Eduardo V. Manalo, for remembering the brethren here in our district, particularly the brethren here in the local congregation of Sugar Land, as well as the Lake Jackson group of [worship service.](https://incmedia.org/our-beliefs/worship-of-god/)

**Edward Pingol:**We will love this place, and we would like to thank, of course, the Church Administration for granting us one of the magnificent property like this.

**Myrtle Alegado:**The purchase of this worship building inspires all Church Of Christ members in Sugar Land and throughout Texas. Standing as a symbol of hope and proof of God’s unwavering love to his people. Myrtle Alegado for the Iglesia Ni Cristo News Network.

**Michael Robinson:**All praises be to the [Almighty God](https://incmedia.org/the-true-god-is-not-three-persons-in-one/). Do you remember the day a house of worship was acquired or dedicated in your part of the world? Tell us in the comments. Well, that does it for this episode of INC News World. Tune in every weekend for new episodes of INC News World on INC TV and our YouTube channel. I’m Michael Robinson. Thank you for joining us. Stay safe and we’ll see you next time. God bless you.

*[On-screen logo graphic]*

*INC NEWS WORLD*

*[On-Screen Text Graphics]*

*INC MEDIA SERVICES*
