## Frequently Asked Questions

### Share

[Ask a Question](/contact/)

### God & Jesus

Is Jesus God?

No. Jesus is not God, according to the Bible.

Watch the [full video for biblical evidence](https://incmedia.org/is-jesus-both-god-and-man/)and the reason many mistake Jesus for God.

Why is the trinity wrong?

The trinity is not a teaching from the Bible. Learn and understand biblical truths that prove the trinity is a man-made teaching.

[Watch the full video](https://incmedia.org/why-god-is-not-a-trinity/)for the biblical answer.

Is there more than one God?

No there’s not. Learn how the Bible teaches that the Father is the only true God.

Watch the [full video for the biblical answer.](https://incmedia.org/why-god-is-not-a-trinity/)

Who is the true God?

Learn how the Bible teaches that the Father is the only true God.

Watch the [full video for the biblical answer.](https://incmedia.org/the-true-god-is-not-three-persons-in-one/)

### The Bible

Why are there so many Bible translations, and which one does the INC use?

The INC references verses from different translations of the Bible in [order to clearly and faithfully convey the pure message of God](https://incmedia.org/why-use-a-different-bible-translation/)in a given language. The Bible has been translated into many versions in different languages, making it accessible to people from different cultures.

### Salvation & Judgement Day

When is the Day of Judgement?

No one (not even the Lord Jesus Christ) knows the day and hour of the end of the world, except for God Himself. However, there are biblical signs that indicate when the end of the world or Judgment Day is near. [Learn and understand how recent events are signs that the return of our Lord Jesus Christ](https://incmedia.org/our-beliefs/the-second-coming-of-christ-and-judgment-day/)or the end of the world is near.

Watch the full video for the biblical answer.

How can I be saved?

Learn God’s instructions and requirements written in the Bible in order to meet His conditions to enter heaven.

Watch the [full video for the biblical answer.](https://incmedia.org/what-is-salvation-why-do-we-need-it/)

### Church Distinctions

Why do you say the Church Of Christ is the one true Church? Won’t all churches be saved?

Not all churches will be saved because not all churches are true. The true Church is not just any church that uses the Bible, but rather the Church that is introduced by the Bible. [Learn what the Bible says about the only Church](https://incmedia.org/does-a-true-church-exist/)that will be saved and how to identify it.

Watch the full video for the biblical answer.

Who is Felix Y. Manalo? Why do you believe Brother Felix Y. Manalo is the last messenger of God?

Felix Y. Manalo is the Last Messenger of God in these last days which was prophesied in the Bible. This can also be identified through the fulfillment of the prophecies concerning his place of origin, the nature of the work entrusted to him, and the time period when he would be commissioned by God to carry out His work. Brother Felix Y. Manalo, who preached God’s pure message regarding the true Church Of Christ, is the fulfillment of those biblical prophecies.

Watch the [full video for the biblical answer](https://incmedia.org/what-we-believe-about-brother-felix-y-manalo/).

### Holidays & Prohibitions

Why is eating blood prohibited?

The Bible clearly and strictly prohibits the eating of blood by his ancient people and in the modern Christian era. This is because God created blood to give life, to also redeem man from sins and not for consumption.

[Watch the full biblical answer here.](https://incmedia.org/is-eating-blood-a-sin/)

Why don’t you observe the Sabbath?

The Sabbath is a command of God recorded in the Bible for a specific people during a specific period of time. The INC firmly believes in and follows the biblical teaching for God’s people living in the Christian era.

Watch the [full video for the biblical answer](https://incmedia.org/is-the-sabbath-still-relevant-today/).

Why are INC members not allowed to date or marry someone outside the Church?

The INC follows the biblical teaching of God for His people not to unite in marriage with un-believers as it would lead them away from Him and to destruction.

[Watch the full biblical answer here.](https://incmedia.org/interfaith-relationships-do-they-work/)

Why doesn’t your Church allow divorce?

The INC firmly believes in and follows the biblical teaching of God on the sanctity of marriage and the prohibition of divorce.

[Watch the full video for the biblical answer.](https://incmedia.org/what-does-the-bible-say-about-divorce/)

### Membership

How do I become a member?

The Lord Jesus Christ is the One who established the process of entering or becoming a member of His Church. A person must first be taught or preached the Gospel by those whom He authorized or sent to preach the gospel. Those who believe in the teachings must be baptized into the Church Of Christ.

The Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ) implements the process established by Christ. A person must follow these steps to become a member:

Learn the basic Bible teachings upheld in the Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ) through Bible Study lessons.
Undergo a probation period during which a person is able to prove that he truly believes through obedience to the teachings of the Bible.
Be baptized into the Church Of Christ.

If you are interested, [we can help you get started to become a member.](https://incmedia.org/become-a-member/)

Submit or email your question and our friendly support team will reply within 24 hours.

## Stories of Faith

## What People Find in This Church

From every Bible study, I felt like in the Church Of Christ, I’m always with family and [there was] always a hug waiting for you, you know? And I was like, “I need to be part of the true Church, not just any church but the true Church. And in the Church Of Christ, I’m there. I’m happy to be here. Tawanda Cochrane ![Woman smiling into camera](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/09/02200237/SOF_S2021_EP20_TAWANDA-COCHRANE_BANNER.jpg)My favorite answered prayer was when we were going through some difficult financial times. I was working three or four different jobs at the time. And I still didn’t really know what I wanted to do with my life. So that’s why I took the police exam not expecting it to go anywhere, but I remembered asking God that, “If this is what you want me to do, please help me to do my best.” So I passed the exam. Kalani Apilado ![man smiling at camera in front of gray backdrop](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/06/15173754/SOF_2023_03_WEB_OTT_BANNER.jpg)My story of faith is…never give up. No matter what you’re going through in life, keep seeking, keep looking, keep praying, and keep praying. Martin Castillo ![Martin smiling into camera](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/09/02201952/2950b1c6-martin-sof-banner-01.jpg)"Now, I see that we need God. We only need Him in our lives, in our families, in the marriages, in our friendships, in order for us to have the happiness … because without Him, there’s no happiness." Marisol Cuahutle ![Woman smiling into camera](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/03/02190914/SOF_2022-03_WEB_OTT_BANNER.jpg)From every Bible study, I felt like in the Church Of Christ, I’m always with family and [there was] always a hug waiting for you, you know? And I was like, “I need to be part of the true Church, not just any church but the true Church. And in the Church Of Christ, I’m there. I’m happy to be here. Tawanda Cochrane ![Woman smiling into camera](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/09/02200237/SOF_S2021_EP20_TAWANDA-COCHRANE_BANNER.jpg)My favorite answered prayer was when we were going through some difficult financial times. I was working three or four different jobs at the time. And I still didn’t really know what I wanted to do with my life. So that’s why I took the police exam not expecting it to go anywhere, but I remembered asking God that, “If this is what you want me to do, please help me to do my best.” So I passed the exam. Kalani Apilado ![man smiling at camera in front of gray backdrop](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/06/15173754/SOF_2023_03_WEB_OTT_BANNER.jpg)

## Guam Declares July 27 as Iglesia Ni Cristo Day

![The group sits and stands in two rows while holding up the Governor’s proclamation document.](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/08/07071808/XL-PIO-GUAM-001-JS-001-copy.jpg)Government officials in Guam formally declare July 27, 2025 INC Day in celebration of the 111th Anniversary.
- [Church News](https://incmedia.org/tag/church-news/)

HAGÅTÑA, Guam—In celebration of the 111th Anniversary of the Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ), government officials in Guam formally declared July 27, 2025, as Iglesia Ni Cristo Day. The Ufisinan I Maga’håga (Office of the Governor) proclaimed it on July 18, 2025.

Governor Lourdes Leon Guerrero signed the official proclamation, recognizing the Iglesia Ni Cristo’s ongoing outreach programs and positive contributions to the island’s communities. “We are very appreciative of the commitment of your Church. We certainly welcome the friendship and the relationship of the Church in our community because these missions improve our families’ lives and make Guam a better place to live.”

Brother Joven Sepillo Jr., Supervising Minister of the Church’s Ecclesiastical District of Micronesia, and fellow ministers of the Church witnessed the signing. Lieutenant Governor Joshua Franquez Tenorio was also present for the occasion.

On behalf of the Iglesia Ni Cristo, Brother Joven expressed sincere gratitude for the proclamation. “The Church Of Christ remains committed to being a positive influence to our community, not only by teaching our fellow members in the Church Of Christ to be good citizens, but most importantly, to teach them to be God-fearing and to be responsible contributors to the growth and the progress of our communities here in the island of Guam,” he said.

The [Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ)](https://directory.iglesianicristo.net/)is a Christian religion in more than 165 countries and territories. It is composed of members from more than 150 different ethnicities and nationalities.

For more about the Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ) and its activities, please visit www.iglesianicristo.net

*Pictures for Press Release:*

Brother Joven Sepillo Jr, Supervising Minister of the Iglesia Ni Cristo (INC) Ecclesiastical District of Micronesia, shakes hands with Lourdes Leon Guerrero, Governor of Guam. Guam Governor Lourdes Leon Guerrero (right) and Lieutenant Governor Joshua Franquez Tenorio (left) with Brother Joven Sepillo Jr., Supervising Minister of the Church’s Ecclesiastical District of Micronesia, and other Church ministers at the signing of the Proclamation of July 27 as “Iglesia Ni Cristo Day” in Guam. Iglesia Ni Cristo ministers from the island of Guam, along with Guam Governor Lourdes Leon Guerrero (seated, center right), with Lieutenant Governor Joshua Franquez Tenorio (seated, center left), and District Supervising brother Joven Sepillo Jr., holding the Proclamation of July 27 as “Iglesia Ni Cristo Day” in Guam. Iglesia Ni Cristo (INC) ministers from the island of Guam pose with brother Joven Sepillo Jr. (center), INC District Supervising Minister of Micronesia, with the Proclamation of July 27 as “Iglesia Ni Cristo Day” in Guam.

## House of Worship Dedicated in Gold Coast, Australia

Witness how the latest Iglesia Ni Cristo, Church Of Christ chapel in Australia reached the day of dedication to God through a special worship service
- [Church News](https://incmedia.org/tag/church-news/)

Pause Play 00:00 08:55 Unmute Mute Disable captions Enable captions Settings Captions Disabled Quality undefined Speed Normal Go back to previous menu Go back to previous menu Go back to previous menu 0.5× 0.75× Normal 1.25× 1.5× 1.75× 2× 4× PIP Exit fullscreen Enter fullscreen Play Pause Play 00:00 08:55 Unmute Mute Disable captions Enable captions Settings Captions Disabled Quality undefined Speed Normal Go back to previous menu Go back to previous menu Go back to previous menu 0.5× 0.75× Normal 1.25× 1.5× 1.75× 2× 4× PIP Exit fullscreen Enter fullscreen Play Play Higher ResolutionPlay Lower Resolution[Download Video](#elementor-action%3Aaction%3Dpopup%3Aopen%26settings%3DeyJpZCI6IjQ5MDMxIiwidG9nZ2xlIjpmYWxzZX0%3D)[Ask a Question](/contact/)Click to expand transcript

*[Show Open]*

**Michael Robinson:**The Gold Coast in Queensland, Australia, is a major tourist destination known for its beautiful beaches and where the sun shines bright. But today, the Gold Coast shines extra bright as a new house of worship was dedicated to God through a special [worship service.](https://incmedia.org/our-beliefs/worship-of-god/)Let’s go to Brendan Thompson with the story.

**Milan Ryan Andrews:**Our first reaction this morning was honestly just out of this world.

**Nene Bell:**So thankful and in gratitude to be able to have this really wonderful house of worship here in Gold Coast.

**Russel Ronquillo:**A great experience that I will cherish till the day I die.

**Brendan Thompson:**These were the reactions of members of the [Church Of Christ](https://incmedia.org/seekers-find-answers-in-the-church-of-christ/)here in Gold Coast after a special worship service where they were able to take part in a momentous event, the dedication of a new house of worship to God. With its subtropical climate, world famous theme parks and stunning beaches, the Gold Coast in Queensland has become one of Australia’s most visited cities, serving for many as a temporary getaway from the stresses of everyday life. For members of the Church of Christ here in the Gold Coast however, there’s a special place where they find true peace and happiness: their newly renovated house of worship, which was dedicated today to the Lord God.

**Russel Ronquillo:**From the start of [hymn singing](https://incmedia.org/experience-hymns-in-the-worship-service/)until the end, the spiritual blessing that we received today nothing can compare. Only proves that our Lord God really loves us.

**Brother Edwil Zabala:**Beloved Brothers and Sisters, fellow members of the Church Of Christ, our Executive Minister, Brother Eduardo Manalo, extends his warmest greetings to all the brethren, not only in the congregation of Gold Coast, but also to the whole district of Queensland. He greatly desires that all of you brethren, not only in this local congregation, but in the entire district, will continue to uphold our divine election and serve the Lord God so as to receive the grace of salvation.

**Brendan Thompson:**Leading in the dedication of the house of worship and sent from the Philippines by the [Church Administration,](https://incmedia.org/our-beliefs/on-the-church-administration/)Brother Edwil Zabala officiated the special worship service. Members were strengthened once again by the lesson they heard.

**Nene Bell:**To understand the importance of being closer to God, knowing that we have a living God.

**Josephine Currie:**Worship Service is very important because it’s our refuge with God. This is where we pray, tell our problems to God, and ask for help in everything we do

**Brother Glenn David:**Prioritize the worship service, because if we forsake our worship service because of problems and challenges in life, just like what was mentioned in our worship service lesson, you’re just adding to your problem. So we don’t want that to happen, to have God missing in our lives. So all the more we should attend a worship service, go to the temple, and all the more [God will be with us.](https://incmedia.org/how-can-one-attain-gods-blessings/)

**Brendan Thompson:**Humility and gratitude were not the only emotions felt during this historic occasion, but for members like Nene, today’s dedication served as a symbol of hope and light during life’s many challenges.

**Nene Bell:**Brother Murray, my husband, was diagnosed with Frontotemporal Dementia. And it’s really with [God’s help](https://incmedia.org/the-type-of-prayer-that-heals-the-sick/)that we’re able to overcome all this struggle, all these tests, one after another, and we’re here and celebrating our dedication of the local [congregation].

**Brendan Thompson:**This historic event was made even more special for church members like Mark, a licensed electrician, who was one of the many brethren who helped in the renovations of the house of worship.

**Mark Henderson:**It did require a lot of time, a lot of extra time and a lot of extra effort. So it’s quite a [substantial upgrade](https://incmedia.org/the-details-that-make-the-building/)to the electrical system. Everyone came together and really pitched in and really helped one another. It was really such a good group effort.

**Milan Ryan Andrews:**A lot of us here have sacrificed so much. Many of the brethren have put so much blood, sweat and tears into this. So to see it all in one piece now and standing up straight, it’s beautiful and it’s really, really, really nice.

**Josephine Currie:**We have to keep our faith with God and look after our house of worship so our God will be glorified.

**Brother Orly Macayan:**The Chapel dedication is very important to all of us, not only here in Gold Coast local but even in the entire district of Queensland, Australia. This means that once again, [we have felt the love and kindness of our Lord God](https://incmedia.org/how-do-we-experience-gods-love/)through our Executive Minister, Brother Eduardo V. Manalo.

**Milan Ryan Andrews:**The House of Worship means everything to us. The place you come to be closest to God and our Lord Jesus Christ. So no other greater place to be.

**Nene Bell:**This is where I find strength and peace of mind. This is where I draw strength and hope. I am really thankful to God and gratitude as well to the Church Administration. I can come down here anytime, utter my personal prayer, and it’s the only through God that I can call on anytime.

**Brother Edwil Zabala:**Aside from pleasing the Lord God, it also helps the members of the Church, because houses of worship are used primarily as places where we offer praise to the Lord God and where we offer our prayers to the Father. It’s a place where a member of the Church of Christ can go even when it’s not a worship service day, to speak to the Lord God, because the Lord God makes a promise to his nation. In the House of Worship dedicated to him by his nation that is where his name will dwell. His eyes, [His ears are open to the prayers](https://incmedia.org/god-answers-prayers/)there. His heart will always be there.

**Russel Ronquillo:**Thank you so much. Through the Church Administration, through Brother Eduardo V. Manalo, all our praises and glory  to our Lord God.

**Brendan Thompson:**This newly renovated house of worship is further proof of God’s love, guidance and help to the Church Of Christ all over the world, and to the Church Administration, he placed to lead us. From Gold Coast Australia. I’m Brother Brendan Thompson, reporting to the Iglesia Ni Cristo News Network.

**Michael Robinson:**Congratulations to the members in Gold Coast, and what a beautiful chapel. Are you waiting for a [house of worship](https://incmedia.org/the-number-of-houses-of-worship-in-the-church-of-christ-has-increased-once-again/)to be dedicated in your part of the world? Tell us in the comments. Well, that does it for this episode of INC News World. Tune in every weekend for new episodes of INC News World on INC TV and our YouTube channel. I’m Michael Robinson, thank you for joining us, stay safe and we’ll see you next time. God bless you.

*[On-screen logo graphic]*

*INC NEWS WORLD*

*[On-Screen Text Graphics]*

## How Do I Find the Right Church for My Family?

- [Christian Relationships](https://incmedia.org/tag/christian-relationships/)

![](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/02/11183924/OPT-NEWBANNER-BLOG-Right-Church-for-fAMILY-1024x576.jpg)[Ask a Question](/contact/)

You’ve decided: You want to raise your family as true Christians and you’re excited about taking the next step to building a closer relationship with God, not only for yourself, but for those closest to your heart. You understand the impact this can have on the peace in your home and you’re looking forward to meeting a Christian community that supports and reinforces your beliefs. But like anything in life, you want to make sure. So you wonder: *How do I find the right church for my family?*Who makes this call and what factors do I have to take into consideration.

In 1979, my wife Sandy and I found ourselves in a similar situation. Both of us were raised Catholic in upstate New York, and we assumed we’d raise our children the exact same way.

![An old family photo with a mom, dad and child sitting in front of a birthday cake.](https://storage.googleapis.com/incmedia2019/2020/02/9ef0999b-bobblog.jpg)

But then I accepted an invitation to learn more about the Church Of Christ.

And as we learned about the teachings followed in the Church Of Christ, we were faced with not only the question of whether this Church was the true Church found in the Bible, but also what would it mean for us to raise our daughter and any future children we may have in this religion?

### A Support System for Every Member of the Family

For more than a hundred years, the Church Of Christ has been a part of many communities, including the most basic unit in society: the family. The foundation of the Christian lifestyle as guided by the Bible is embodied in the Church’s three Christian Family Organizations: a group for those who are married, a group for unmarried adults, and a youth group for those baptized and under the age of 18.

The Church Of Christ understands that finding the right Christian church isn’t only about you — it’s about the potential spiritual growth of those around you as well. These segmented groups were designed to do the following:

- Create a support system for members who are going through similar experiences in varying phases of their lives
- Receive spiritual counsel on how to get through specific phases of their lives, and how to pursue the next steps in their personal and spiritual growth
- Strengthen brotherhood in the Church through activities designed for their peer group

Aside from these core initiatives, what’s great about these Christian Family Organizations is that every activity they do is wholesome and geared towards glorifying God. Church Of Christ activities enrich the individual Church member and even the community through activities of service and outreach.

### The Importance of Attending Worship Service

In the Church Of Christ we love to be at our [houses of worship and chapels](https://incmedia.org/blueprint/), and especially to attend worship services. We worship once on the weekends and once in the middle of the week. The structure of both worship services is the same: prayer, hymn singing, listening to the words of God, and offering.

Attending worship service is important to us. To a true Christian, the importance of worshipping God isn’t something that’s up for negotiation or debate. It’s a teaching of God, and there’s no place we’d rather be. It’s also an opportunity for us to hear the words of God which we need to grow spiritually.

Many members even take notes during worship service and jot down reminders for themselves as they study the words of God.

![A worship service setting with a group of people looking at a screen holding a pen and paper.](https://storage.googleapis.com/incmedia2019/2020/02/6eb6f5f1-lx6c9833-1024x683.jpg)

There are also many activities held at either the house of worship or at designated worship locations, most of which are open to the community, and all are geared towards spiritual enrichment. Here’s what to expect in terms of activities during the week:

- Bible studies — attended by both Bible students and church members looking to deepen their [understanding of the core teachings](https://incmedia.org/study-with-us/)inside the Church Of Christ
- Community service and other social events — a more relaxed setting geared towards getting to [know our community](https://incmedia.org/young-christians-appreciating-teachers/)a little better, as well as introducing ourselves and answering any questions about our beliefs.
- Meetings — we regularly have meetings for the different Christian Family Organizations to discuss upcoming activities, cover relevant topics, and connect with individuals who may be going through similar experiences.

### Laying a Solid Moral Foundation

Throughout the years we’ve seen how “so-called” Christian churches have bent with the times in order to sustain their pew count or project an appearance that’s more palatable to the masses and what’s socially acceptable.

Since its establishment, the [teachings in the Church Of Christ](https://incmedia.org/our-beliefs/)haven’t changed – not even a little bit. All the teachings within the Church are based on the Bible and it’s what members use as their guide in navigating their Christian family lifestyles, as well as strengthening their individual relationships with God.

![A family outdoors with their son’s outstretched arms to his dad.](https://storage.googleapis.com/incmedia2019/2020/02/13aaeff1-img_0234-edit-copy-1024x576.jpg)

We don’t take away, we don’t add — as is instructed by God Himself. It’s why the members of the Church Of Christ have a universal culture about them. Some say we’re friendly, we’re nice, and we’re welcoming people. It’s because no matter where you go to visit us on this planet, we all believe the same teachings, and we live by it.

### Raising our Children with the words of God

The youngest members of the Christian Family Organization attend the Children’s Worship Service (CWS), a worship gathering formatted specifically for a young Christian. In the Church Of Christ, going to worship service is an essential part of a Christian child’s life.

![A worship service setting with a handful of people sitting on pews and a child smiling and looking at the woman next to her.](https://storage.googleapis.com/incmedia2019/2020/02/71fafb41-jxs00004-copy-1024x576.jpg)

[Raising children in the Church](https://incmedia.org/3-times-my-kids-asked-is-the-bible-real/)starts in our homes. Before our children reach the age of four, when they can understand the lessons taught in Children’s Worship Service or CWS, we, as Church Of Christ parents, are already praying with our children, and talking about God and our Lord Jesus Christ. By the time they are old enough to attend the worship service on their own, they already understand essential parts of the structure:

- Prayer
- Singing hymns of praises
- Active participation in the lesson

These gatherings are integral to raising Christian children because, since childhood, they are able to recognize what is expected of them by God, as well as seek Him out as a constant in their life. In addition to learning God’s words, young children will benefit from learning:

- Proper manners — they learn this and practice it with one another
- Leadership skills – Once a young member is mature enough, they’ll be encouraged to help in the Children’s Worship Service. The CWS is led by youth group members who are not only active members of the Church, they are well trained, and pretty good human beings in general.
- Being Christian is cool. They’re always kept busy with fun, wholesome activities and when they’re at home, the words of God are reinforced with Christian media and programming created just for them (and found right here on incmedia.org!).

## We’re Happy You’re Here!

The only way to know if a church is for you is to take the first step. 41 years later, Sandy and I are still glad we did.

![A smiling couple against a black background](https://storage.googleapis.com/incmedia2019/2020/02/2cbc26b3-img_0209-copy-2-1024x684.jpg)

The Church Of Christ welcomes walk-ins, but if you’d be more comfortable calling first and having someone meet you or guide you in, we’d be happy to do that too!

Here are some important links for you to bookmark as you prepare for your first visit:

- [Contact us](https://incmedia.org/study-with-us/)here so we can connect you with a guide
- Or give us your contact Infoand we’ll get in touch with you to personally answer any of your questions about the big day.
- Find a [local congregation](https://directory.iglesianicristo.net/)near you

No matter who you are, where you’re from, what your past was—we’re happy you’ve made steps towards searching for the right church for your family and building a stronger relationship with God.

We’ll see you soon!

- Name * First Last
- I want to * learn what God can do for my family. learn teachings taught inside the Church Of Christ. talk to a Minister about my family. I'm not sure yet, but please contact me.
- Best Email to Reach You *

### About the Author(S)

## Robert Pellien

Brother Robert (Bob) Pellien has been an ordained minister in the Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ) for over 30 years. He grew up a Catholic in Upstate New York. After joining the Air Force in the 1970s, he found himself wondering if there was more to life than motorcycles and scuba diving. It was at that time he was introduced to the Church Of Christ, where the clear and definite Bible-based answers in the worship services caught his attention.

After getting baptized, he and his wife moved to Florida where he was later called into the ministry. Since then he has been assigned to lead congregations of the Church Of Christ around the world. Brother Bob is currently the host of the show That’s in the Bible and INC International Edition.

[All Posts](https://incmedia.org/writer/robert-pellien/)

## How Do I know God Is Listening To My Prayers?

How do we know if God hears our prayers? Learn three reasons why God may not be listening to your prayers and how you can build a relationship with God.
- [Prayer](https://incmedia.org/tag/prayer/)

![Man pondering looking out into the ocean.](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/11/02203055/21dd228d-man-pondering-wondering-about-his-prayers-1024x576.jpg)[Ask a Question](/contact/)

Prayer is such an important aspect of our lives. It helps us find courage in times of need. It gives us that boost of confidence and reassurance that we’re not alone in what we’re about to face.

I remember when I was 14 years old, and it was my first day at a new school. Our family had moved into a new house in a new neighborhood far away from where I grew up. I had no friends to sit with during lunch, and no one to walk beside on the way home. But what I did have was my prayers. Every day I would sit in the library, eat my sandwich, and pray for Him to stick by me.

For all of us,  prayer is needed not only during these small quiet moments in life—we pray right before some of the biggest ones, too. We pray before entering into all kinds of high-stress competitive environments. We see athletes pray before a big sporting match, and performers pray before taking center stage. [Even pro-gamers pray](https://incmedia.org/keeping-my-eyes-on-the-prize/).

And it also allows us to find the small pockets of peace that we need to help deal with our hectic lives. [According to research](https://www.healthline.com/nutrition/12-benefits-of-meditation#section4), prayer and meditation help enhance self-awareness and focus. It’s like our secret weapon in fighting whatever the world has to throw at us.

But sometimes, it can feel like our prayers go unanswered.  Like the world around us is beating us down, drowning our pleading voice. With every prayer unanswered we tend to lose belief that we can do better and be better. We begin to forget or take for granted the things that matter most because we’re frustrated by the silence. And, eventually, we start asking [“Is God even listening to me?”](https://incmedia.org/does-god-hear-me/)Or, “Why hasn’t God answered my prayers?”

#### Some have blamed God for not answering a prayer.

## We need it, but does prayer work?

It’s gotten so bad that some people have not only started to give up on prayer but have [given up on believing](https://www.reddit.com/r/AskReddit/comments/91sjjq/why_dont_you_believe_in_god/)that God exists. Many have grown up in households where [parents don’t believe in God](https://religionnews.com/2016/09/22/why-most-people-leave-religion-they-just-stop-believing/), so they ask, “Why bother praying?” Others who did grow up in religious homes have stopped having faith altogether. Worse of all, some have [blamed God for not answering a prayer](https://www.buzzfeed.com/ajanibazile/reasons-people-stopped-believing-in-god)for something important, feeling like He didn’t save them from the repercussions of a bad decision they’ve made.

#### I have some good news for you. Prayer does work. It really does.

Well, if you’re reading this right now, I have some good news for you. Prayer does work. [It really does](https://incmedia.org/rewa-thompson-my-favorite-answered-prayer/). And studies show that [most people believe there is a higher power](https://www.pewforum.org/2018/04/25/when-americans-say-they-believe-in-god-what-do-they-mean/)up there watching and guiding us. But the key is in order for Him to hear us, we need to know what kind of prayer He will listen to.

## Not all prayers are the same.

That’s right, not all prayers are the same, and not all prayers will be answered if you’re not praying the right way. So let’s go down the list of some of the reasons why [God might not be answering you,](https://incmedia.org/does-god-hear-me/)based on what I’ve learned in group Bible studies.

#### 1. You’re asking for the wrong thing.

**“And when you ask, you do not receive it, because your motives are bad; you ask for things to use for your own pleasures.”****James 4:3 GNT**

Just because it’s something we want doesn’t always mean it’s something we need. And God always knows what we need, because He’s always looking out for us. So the next time we get on our knees to pray, make sure it’s for something that will bring us closer to God and benefit us in our spiritual journey.

#### We begin to second guess the answer God gave us which can lead to second-guessing if prayer works.

#### 2. Your prayer was answered, you just don’t like the answer.

**“Trust in the LORD with all your heart, And lean not on your own understanding; In all your ways acknowledge Him, And He shall direct your paths.”****Proverbs 3:5-6 NKJV**

Face it, sometimes we pray for an answer, for a sign, for some divine guidance, and the prayer is answered! Sometimes almost right away. But when we see it in front of us and realize that this is what God wants us to do, we refuse to accept the answer. We begin to second guess the answer God gave us which can lead to second-guessing if prayer works because we never get the answer we want. We analyze the wording of our prayers, too, hoping to find a loophole to what we prayed for and how we said it in order for us to justify ignoring the answer we got.

#### The absolute best place to pray and build that relationship with God is within a house of worship.

### 3. Your relationship with God needs work.

**“The Lord is close to every person who calls to him for help. The Lord is close to every person who truly worships him.”****Psalms 145: 18  ETRV**

Like any healthy relationship, our relationship with God needs constant effort. We have to be willing to not just ask Him for things but show Him our willingness to sacrifice and obey His teachings. And while we can close our eyes and pray almost anywhere, the absolute best place to pray and build that relationship is within a [house of worship](http://iglesianicristo.net/directory/), during a [worship service](http://iglesianicristo.net/directory/). It is there that we can truly connect with Him and His teachings and where we can openly and honestly plead to Him and let our worries be known.

#### This right here is an answer to your prayers.

## Your prayers were answered, you just didn’t know it.

If you’ve gotten this far in the blog post, and are taking the time to truly find out why [God hasn’t answered your prayers](https://incmedia.org/does-god-hear-me/), it’s apparent that you value establishing a true and long-lasting relationship with God.

And with that relationship comes the right to pray to Him and worship Him in truth along with others who feel and hope and yearn for the same things you do.

I eventually graduated valedictorian at the new school I moved to when I was 14. I was surrounded by friends cheering me on as I accepted my award, and in my speech, I remembered to thank God for answering my prayers, and for always being by my side. So this right here might be an answer to your prayers, it’s what you have been searching for all your life.

Don’t let your future prayers go unanswered. With that said, I leave you with one last Bible verse I learned in a worship service, Psalms. 37:34 TLB:

**“Don’t be impatient for the Lord to act! Keep traveling steadily along his pathway and in due season he will honor you with every blessing,…”**

![1. Psalms 37:34 TLB with a clock and a pink and blue background.](https://storage.googleapis.com/incmedia2019/2019/11/ad483aae-psalms37.jpg)

####

About the Author:

This blog was co-written by Martin Zerrudo with biblical support by Richard Juatco, a Minister of the Gospel of the Church Of Christ.

Martin Zerrudo is a Producer at INCMEDIA Services, a lifetime gamer and a Christian. He currently hosts a podcast called [“Heart & Soul”](https://incmedia.org/heart-and-soul/)tackling subjects regarding Christian relationships and Christian living.

Richard Juatco has been an ordained minister in the Church Of Christ (Iglesia Ni Cristo) for five years.

## Pasos Importantes Para Llegar al Cielo

## Iglesia Ni Cristo (INC) completes renovation, dedicates worship building in Norfolk, Virginia

![Aerial image of the property containing a steepled chapel, other buildings, landscaping and a parking lot.](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/23114033/XL-PIO-THMB-VA_NORFOLK-001-JS-001-copy.jpg)The Church Of Christ dedicates its worship building in Norfolk, Virginia after extensive renovations.

NORFOLK, Virginia – A newly-renovated worship building of the Iglesia Ni Cristo  (INC) or Church Of Christ was dedicated on September 7, 2024. The dedication was officiated by Brother Edward Maranan, Coordinating Minister of the INC’s United States East Office.

Formerly owned by Zion Grace United Methodist Church, the chapel was sold to the Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ) on June 15, 2018 for $650,000.

This 230-seater worship building located at 1440 W Little Creek Rd, Norfolk, VA 23505 underwent a complete remodel of its exterior and interior, audio visual wall panel, AC unit installation and parking lot repavement. The total cost of the renovation was $720,535.01, all coming from the voluntary contributions of its Church members.

INC Members in Norfolk were once part of the Virginia Beach congregation, but due to the Church’s exponential growth, the Norfolk Congregation was established to accommodate the growing number of INC members from the Hampton Roads region. *“Beginning with the Virginia Beach congregation, we had an extension in Richmond and Newport News, which became a congregation,”*Brother Charles Howlett, a deacon, explained. *“They had so many brethren there that they decided to make a Chesapeake congregation and a Norfolk congregation.”*

Brother Kevin Peck, another deacon, added, *“The dedication of this chapel today is because our Executive Minister saw a vision for this property, allowed us to obtain this property, and we worked hard to refurbish it to become a completely revitalized, renovated worship building, and I think the community really appreciated that.”*

Supervising Minister of Virginia, Brother Esteban Inocencio Jr. explained the significance of dedicating this house of worship. “ *Inside the house of worship is where we [INC Members] hear and are taught the teachings and commandments of God, where we are taught how to please Him,*” he said.

Seeing the renovated worship building also made an impact in the surrounding community, as a resident remarked, *“Thank you for making the neighborhood look better.”*

The Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ) is a Christian religion in more than 165 countries and territories. It is composed of members from more than 150 different ethnicities and nationalities.

For more about the Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ) and its activities,
please visit www.iglesianicristo.net

[The newly renovated house of worship of the Iglesia Ni Cristo in the congregation of Norfolk.](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/23114704/PIO-2024_NorfolkVADedication_1-scaled.jpg)[Iglesia Ni Cristo (INC) choir members in a special worship gathering on September 7, 2024 for the dedication of Norfolk congregation’s house of worship.](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/23114720/PIO-2024_NorfolkVADedication_2-scaled.jpg)[Brother Edward Maranan, Coordinating Minister of the United States East Office of the Iglesia Ni Cristo (INC), reading the letter from INC Executive Minister, Brother Eduardo V. Manalo.](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/23114738/PIO-2024_NorfolkVADedication_3-scaled.jpg)[Iglesia Ni Cristo (INC) members gather for a special worship service for the dedication of Norfolk congregation’s house of worship.](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/23114752/PIO-2024_NorfolkVADedication_4-scaled.jpg)[Brother Edward Maranan, Coordinating Minister of the United States East Office of the Iglesia Ni Cristo (INC), preaches the Bible-based lesson during
the special worship service for the dedication of Norfolk congregation’s house of worship on September 7, 2024.](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/23114805/PIO-2024_NorfolkVADedication_5-scaled.jpg)[From the rear of the worship space, the congregation faces forward to the preacher and choir listening intently.](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/23114818/PIO-2024_NorfolkVADedication_6-scaled.jpg)[Iglesia Ni Cristo (INC) members in overflow area, listen intently during a special worship gathering for the dedication of Norfolk congregation’s house of worship.](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/23114830/PIO-2024_NorfolkVADedication_7-scaled.jpg)[Iglesia Ni Cristo (INC) Ministers of the Ecclesiastical District of Virginia, with their respective wives, pose for a photo.](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/23114842/PIO-2024_NorfolkVADedication_8-scaled.jpg)[Some youth members of the Iglesia Ni Cristo (INC) choir after the dedication of the Norfolk house of worship on September 7, 2024.](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/23114854/PIO-2024_NorfolkVADedication_9-scaled.jpg)[Iglesia Ni Cristo (INC) Ministers and INC members are shown here after the worship gathering at the newly renovated Norfolk house of worship on September 7, 2024.](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/23114910/PIO-2024_NorfolkVADedication_10-scaled.jpg)[Iglesia Ni Cristo (INC) Officers are seen here after the worship gathering at the newly renovated Norfolk house of worship on September 7, 2024.](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/23114926/PIO-2024_NorfolkVADedication_11-scaled.jpg)[The newly renovated house of worship of the Norfolk congregation of the Iglesia Ni Cristo (INC).](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/23114937/PIO-2024_NorfolkVADedication_12-scaled.jpg)[The podium chairs in the newly renovated house of worship of the Iglesia Ni Cristo’s Norfolk congregation.](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/23114951/PIO-2024_NorfolkVADedication_13-scaled.jpg)[One of the center chandeliers in the newly renovated house of worship of the Iglesia Ni Cristo’s Norfolk congregation.](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/23115003/PIO-2024_NorfolkVADedication_14-scaled.jpg)[The newly renovated house of worship of the Iglesia Ni Cristo in the congregation of Norfolk.](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/23114033/XL-PIO-THMB-VA_NORFOLK-001-JS-001-copy.jpg)

## Iglesia Ni Cristo promotes kindness through series of ‘INC Giving’ projects in British Columbia

![Police woman smiling as young boy gives her flowers and a thank you card](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/03/28172051/PIO_BC_INCG_THUMBNAIL_web.jpg)The Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ), with active members in 165 countries, extends kindness in various parts of the globe to uplift people’s lives amid the lingering effects of the pandemic

**BRITISH COLUMBIA, Canada –**A string of successful community outreach events under the [INC Giving project of the Iglesia Ni Cristo (INC)](https://incgiving.org/)or Church Of Christ extended acts of kindness in various communities in British Columbia, Canada.

INC members and volunteers, both young and old, shared their time and energy in the various INC Giving projects to benefit their communities.

In February alone, [the INC Giving events that were conducted in British Columbia](https://incmedia.org/beautification-projects-and-coastal-cleanups/)included Community Clean-up Drives in the following areas: Vedder Rotary Loop (Abbotsford-Chilliwack), Marine Drive (Burnaby), Hammond Community Park (Maple Ridge), Queens Park (New Westminster), E-59th Avenue Vancouver (Richmond), TE Scott Park (Surrey, Langley), and Muddy Bay (Delta).

The INC Giving project also included the launching of these successful events: Singing For a Cause at Eagle Ridge Manor in New Westminster, and the Elderly Appreciation at Sunrise Senior Living Reminiscence in North Vancouver.

Under the INC Giving banner, INC volunteers and members also delivered boxes of towels, women’s underclothes, and other essentials such as socks, sweaters, hoodies, brushes and combs to the Downtown Eastside Women’s Centre in Vancouver West.

There was also a Police Appreciation Day held in Abbotsford.

Every INC Giving project was done with thorough planning to ensure the success and safety of those involved.

Cheril Buenavista, the Life Enrichment Manager who organizes and implements activities for the residents at Sunrise Senior Living (Reminiscence) said that they were “astounded” that such projects are being led by a church organization. She also appreciated how the INC Giving volunteers were able to extend kindness and joy, including little children who had singing performances during the INC Giving event for the elderly. She said that their elderly residents “had fun singing the songs too as they were familiar with the songs” performed by the young INC volunteers. She said they were “very grateful” for the gifts that brought joy to their senior residents.

Volunteers, on the other hand, were happy to spread kindness to their communities. Meeting new people, and having opportunities for creativity and personal growth, aside from having fun, were a plus. They are happy to share even little acts of kindness to make people feel valued and empowered.

“As a young adult, giving back to the community is always such a rewarding feeling. There’s never a moment of regret when you give back to people especially when they’re in need,” said GenZ Martina Sandal from Vancouver West during their visit to the Downtown Eastside Women’s Shelter.

“I think it’s important for the kids and the youth to start participating in these activities so that they have a deeper understanding of what it’s like to help others, as well as learning good qualities such as kindness, patience and resilience,” she added.

Studies have shown how much the pandemic has affected the people in British Columbia.

Loneliness and social isolation have been increasingly recognized as a growing public health priority with extensive evidence that these issues take a significant toll on population health. Despite this escalating urgency, exacerbated by the COVID-19 pandemic, effective evidence-based interventions for mitigating social isolation and loneliness are lacking. Based on previous research, this project proposes “acts of kindness” as an antidote to social disconnection.

The INC District Supervising Minister of the [ecclesiastical district of British Columbia](https://directory.iglesianicristo.net/districts/western-canada), Brother Glendo H. Baitan noted how “volunteering by means of giving back to the community is very beneficial socially and emotionally.”

“It helps a person have a sense of self-worth. Most especially, it strengthens their faith and spiritual well-being, because in such activities they can see God’s teachings that are being taught to us by the Church Administration being practiced,” he added.

WHENEVER WE POSSIBLY CAN, DO GOOD TO THOSE WHO NEED IT

*The INC Giving Project is a project of the Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ) that encourages volunteers to go into their communities and share their faith through acts of kindness.*

*Through outreach initiatives, community service projects, and philanthropic contributions, volunteers around the world have joined together to make a positive social impact by offering time and resources for the well-being of those in need.*

*###*

*About the Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ)*

*For more about the Church and its activities, please visit*[www.iglesianicristo.net](http://www.iglesianicristo.net/)*or*[https://incmedia.org/press-room/](https://incmedia.org/press-room/)

Young INC Giving volunteers created cards and artwork to express their gratitude and appreciation to the police officers of the Abbotsford Police Department. INC Giving volunteers in Maple Ridge, BC cleaned up Hammond Community Park as part of a Park Cleanup event In Vancouver, INC Giving volunteers gathered winter clothing to donate to the residents of the Downtown Eastside Women’s Shelter. A Park Cleanup was held at T.E. Scott Park in Surrey, BC to beautify the community surroundings. In New Westminster, BC, young INC Giving volunteers sang for the senior residents of Eagle Ridge Manor to uplift and spread joy to their community. In New Westminster, BC, young INC Giving volunteers sang for the senior residents of Eagle Ridge Manor to uplift and spread joy to their community.

## INC Central Temple: History, Architecture, and Worship Experience

The Iglesia Ni Cristo Central Temple in Quezon City, Philippines is one of the most recognizable examples of religious architecture in the Philippines. It is the centerpiece of the Church and is a shining example of INC architectural style that highlights the legacy of the late Brother Erano G. Manalo. As we reflect on the end of the centennial birth anniversary of Bro. Erano G. Manalo, we celebrate the history of the INC Central Temple, its architectural and spiritual significance, and what makes it such a religious landmark in the Philippines.
- [Christian Living](https://incmedia.org/tag/christian-living/), [Christian Relationships](https://incmedia.org/tag/christian-relationships/)

Play Higher ResolutionPlay Lower Resolution[Download Video](#elementor-action%3Aaction%3Dpopup%3Aopen%26settings%3DeyJpZCI6IjQ5MDMxIiwidG9nZ2xlIjpmYWxzZX0%3D)[Ask a Question](/contact/)Click to expand transcript

**INC Central Temple: History, Architecture, and Worship Experience**

**Nan:**The Philippines has become a tourist hotspot, especially because of the amazing natural beauty of its beaches and coasts. Some would say it has become a bucket list destination for nature lovers. So naturally, when planning an epic trip to the Philippines, most people plan to make any one of these places the first place that they’ll go to. But if you ask me, there’s one destination at the very top of my list–the INC Central Temple.

**Nan:**It’s definitely a bucket list destination for any member of the Church Of Christ. But before the largest house of worship of the Church Of Christ was constructed. Believe it or not, this was all open land. The land that the Central Temple was constructed on was relatively isolated and undeveloped at the time. It was rugged and mountainous terrain. So naturally the land was uneven.

**Nan:**This made the construction process more challenging. Some portions had to be excavated, while others were filled to create a level surface. The Central Temple was built on a 3.95 acre property located on Commonwealth Avenue in Quezon City, Philippines. The local congregation of Templo Central was formerly known as the local Congregation of Balara, which was established in 1948. During the time of The Messenger and Executive Minister at the time, Brother Felix Y. Manalo.

**Nan:**The first worship service was conducted in the home of Sister Maria Bautista. Over time, with the rapid growth of the local Congregation of Balara, various worship venues were established. In the 1970s, the worship services were transferred to the INC Central Complex and the structures that came to be known as Kapilya one and Kapilya two were established.

**Nan:**On May 9th, 1971 the local congregation, known as Balara, would be called the local congregation of Central. The number of brethren in the local congregation of Central rapidly grew, so the Church Administration decided in 1981 to construct a large and modern worship building. This is now known as the Central Temple. Before becoming one singular congregation, it was originally composed of four local congregations–Culiat, Central one and Central two. These local congregations were merged into one and became the local congregation of Templo Central.

**Nan:**So much history. It’s amazing looking back at the growth that led to the construction of this extremely important [place of worship](https://incmedia.org/restoring-faith-and-community-in-philadelphia/). Just as with any structure that has stood the test of time, a solid foundation is needed. And what the Central Temple was constructed on is truly solid.

**Nan:**The Central Temple was constructed on a solid rock foundation, and the site’s bedrock is made of durable Adobe. Adobe is resistant to pests and decay, able to withstand harsh weather  conditions. This solid base was deemed ideal for the temple because of its stability and high elevation. The worship building itself has a footprint of 12,040ft², with its foundation elevated roughly 23ft above the road, and it has a total height of 285ft, from the ground to the tip of its center tower.

**Nan:**The construction of the Central Temple began in March of 1982, and took two years and nearly four months to complete. The truth is, this was a relatively short time period considering the overall size of the Central Temple. However, through God’s mercy and guidance, it was completed with a beautiful and magnificent structure within the appointed time. In commemoration of the Church’s 70th anniversary, the Central Temple was dedicated to God. On July 27, 1984, in a worship service officiated by Brother Erano G. Manalo, the Executive Minister of the Church Of Christ at the time.

**Nan:**This neo-Gothic inspired architectural edifice was designed by the renowned architect Mr. Carlos Antonio Santos Viola.

**Carlos Antonio Santos Viola:**When I got started with the design of this recent temple, I always bear in mind to design, or arrive at a, sort of, a type of architecture that will identify the Church Of Christ, the Iglesia Ni Cristo, that it cannot be mistaken for any other sect except, that it is Iglesia.

**Carlos Antonio Santos Viola:**The moment you see it. You can identify it, since it’s going to be a house of worship for God. Therefore, I have to create a style that will be suited and adaptable for such kind of purpose. So you will notice why, the temple, the, the style or the type is defined, the tendency is for verticality. You will notice in the towers, the spires are all going up, pointing up to heaven. And that’s what they consider the spirituality of this style.

**Nan:**And these design elements have carried over throughout the decades in the design and construction of [INC houses of worship](https://incmedia.org/the-return-to-worship-buildings/)around the world. The tendency of verticality can be clearly seen in every house of worship, through the spires, towers, and even exterior molding designs.

**Nan:**The Central Temple, as the name implies, is the centerpiece of the INC, so it is fitting that this monumental place of worship receives regular additions and updates. Throughout the years, we’ve seen the introduction of groundbreaking tech and audio/video that made its way into houses of worship around the world. It’s a common sight to see large screens in an INC sanctuary, but the Central Temple was the first to utilize a large format screen for regular use during worship services.

**Nan:**On July 23, 2005, during the Thanksgiving worship service for the 91st anniversary of the Church. The digital video wall was used for the first time in the Central Temple. Another important and very significant addition was the installation of a pipe organ. The extensive assembly and installation further enhanced the appearance, and most importantly, the sound quality of the organ.

**Nan:**As an organist and choir director, Emilie Juatco had the chance to visit the Central Temple various times throughout the years.

**Emilie Juatco:**A few times I was able to see the Temple; instances when we were called to attend conference[s]. So we were there to learn things as Choir Directors and taking care of the Choir Members. And usually it is in the vicinity, in the Temple.

**Emilie Juatco:**But we go inside and it’s amazing, you know, it is, a temple is really a very, very different, chapel. There are times when they will call all Choir Directors, and usually it’s always in the Central. And [at] one of our conferences, they decided that all Choir Directors will sing in one worship service. We were so happy, you know, to be included because, I believe at the time, there were 400 of us Choir Directors.

**Emilie Juatco:**It is a very, very different experience, you know, because singing with that many voices, it’s really very uplifting. It’s very, very spiritual, you know, to sing in the Temple, that big chapel that you are giving glory and worshiping God.

**Nan:**TJ joined the Church Of Christ in 2019 and was blessed with a once in a lifetime chance to be baptized in the Philippines.

**Terence Julian:**It was before we were baptized. We went to service in Central Chapel and that was an experience. Just the size of it alone was amazing. That was very humbling. First time I’ve ever been in a place that felt that way.

**Terence Julian:**Seeing all the rows and all the brethren that were attending. I’ve been to a few government buildings that were pretty big and pretty significant here in the States, but nothing compared to going to the Central [Temple]. It was definitely a very unique experience. One of the most memorable experiences. I travel a lot for work, even, as far as Turkey, more specifically in Istanbul.

**Terence Julian:**I’ve visited these, other historical sites have also been to, the castles in Afghanistan that were built by Alexander the Great, essentially. So very significant places. The difference of going to the Central [Temple] and the feeling there, it had much more of a reverence.

**Nan:**And like many travelers, Cuong Doan had a bucket list destination that he and his wife looked forward to visiting.

**Cuong Doan:**My first experience in attending at the Central Temple was actually back in 2006. My wife and I were actually on our honeymoon, so we’re able to attend there. And my first impression, seeing it in person, is just, pictures do not do it justice. And seeing it in person [and] be[ing] completely in awe. It just hits different. It’s important to go there, only because obviously it’s a bucket list thing to do right?

**Cuong Doan:**You can see it, through magazines, see it through social media. Obviously, there are many different social platforms that the Church has, through [incmedia.org](http://incmedia.org), [iglesianicristo.net](http://iglesianicristo.net). But to be there in person, is just, it’s inspiring. If you really want, a true sense of elevating your faith. I think that’s really the place to go, because you could completely feel that when you worship in the Central Temple.

**Emilie Juatco:**You know, the house of worship inside the Church is so important. Although you can pray to God wherever you are. But this, we call this a [house of God](https://incmedia.org/the-framework/). So when you come in, even though it’s not time of worship service, you sit or you kneel and you talk to God, you can feel him. We are so happy, you know, that we have one temple in the Church that we can say is our temple.

**Nan:**It’s been such a privilege to learn about the INC Central Temple. There’s no doubt that this centerpiece of the INC will continue to be a beacon of hope and pride for members of the Church Of Christ all around the world.

**Nan:**The next time you plan a trip to the Philippines, I hope you find your way to Commonwealth Avenue in Quezon City, Philippines to see this magnificent display of engineering and architecture. But more importantly, I hope you experience how powerfully spiritual attending a worship service is in the Central Temple of the Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ). Thanks for joining me on this episode of Blueprint, where in the end, everything is part of God’s plan.

## INC Dedicates Spokane House of Worship After Renovation

![The white exterior of the House of Worship of Spokane with a green lawn.](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/08091244/XL-PIO-THMB-SPOKANE_WA-001-JS-001-copy.jpg)House of Worship in Spokane, Washington was rededicated to God after recent renovations to its interior and exterior.
- [Church News](https://incmedia.org/tag/church-news/)

SPOKANE, Washington — In the heart of the Pacific Northwest, the Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ) has dedicated its house of worship in Spokane, Washington, reaffirming its commitment to serving the community and glorifying the Almighty God. This worship building is one of 22 locations of the Church in Washington State and has been the home of the Local Congregation of Spokane since 2003. Recently, it has undergone significant renovations. The interior was redesigned to increase seating capacity by 20%, with updated finishes and fresh paint applied throughout both the interior and exterior.

Brother Edward Maranan, Minister of the Gospel, led the dedication worship service on September 14, 2025. During the service, he read a letter of congratulations from the Church Administration to the congregation for completing the renovations. He encouraged the brethren to use the house of worship as a place to share the words of God with the broader community.

Members expressed deep appreciation for the improvements and are excited about the prospect of sharing their faith with those in the area. “It feels like a blessing just walking into the chapel. It’s amazing to see how much improvement has been made,” said Claridel Victoria, a member from nearby Burien. “When you enter that sanctuary, you can feel the Holy Spirit. When you need hope, when you need your faith to be strengthened, you can come here and pray,” added Julio Gallegos, a choir member of the Spokane local congregation.

The newly renovated chapel is now open to all who wish to attend worship services located at 3405 W. Bismarck Ave., Spokane, WA 99205:

Wednesday          7:30 pm

Thursday               7:30 pm

Sunday                   9:00 am

Sunday                 11:30 am (Children’s Worship Service)

The Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ) is a Christian religion in more than 160 countries and territories.

For more about the Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ) and its activities,

please visit [www.iglesianicristo.net](http://www.iglesianicristo.net/).

P hotos Courtesy of Iglesia Ni Cristo Public Information Office:

Members of the congregation fill the recently renovated house of worship of the Iglesia Ni Cristo in Spokane, Washington. The seating capacity of the chapel’s interior was increased by 20 percent during the renovation. Brother Edward Maranan, Minister of the Gospel, reads a summary of the renovation accomplishments, history of the local congregation, as well as a letter of congratulations from the Church Administration of the Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ) during this special occasion. Members of the Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ) congregation in Spokane, Washington, alongside members of the nearby community, attend the special dedication worship service to thank the Almighty God for the successful chapel renovation. Members of the congregation of Spokane, Washington fill the newly renovated house of worship of the Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ). Brother Edward Maranan, Minister of the Gospel, leads the congregation in the special worship service thanking the Almighty God for the success and blessing of a renovated house of worship. Ministers of the Gospel and members from congregations of the Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ) around Washington State, along with members of the Church who traveled across the state to witness the special occasion, pose together for a group photo on the property of the Church in Spokane.

## ‘INC Giving’ extends acts of kindness in High River, Alberta

![PHOTO PHOTO INCGiving Volunteers and Guests INCGiving volunteer handing out free packages during an INCGiving event in High River, Alberta. Volunteer handing out free care packages at a community outreach event](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/03/28173835/1920x1080_calgary.PR_.banner.jpg)Residents in High River, Alberta grateful for free care packages in community outreach by Iglesia Ni Cristo

HIGH RIVER, AB, CANADA – Promoting kindness, doing good, and helping others through acts of giving. These are among the goals of the [INC Giving project of the Iglesia Ni Cristo (INC)](https://incgiving.org/)or Church Of Christ, which were once more put into action in a community outreach event in [High River, Alberta, Canada.](https://directory.iglesianicristo.net/districts/canada-v)

Attendees of the INC Giving outreach at the Bob Snodgrass Recreation Complex on February 18 were treated to a message of inspiration, and were given care packages that contained dry goods and other necessities.

The INC members and volunteers in the area acknowledged and thanked guests for coming to the event despite their busy schedule.

“We hope that these care packages can somehow alleviate the stress that anyone in our community may be experiencing in their everyday life,” said Mylene Hementera, a member of the INC.

[The Iglesia Ni Cristo aims to build positive relationships with communities](https://incmedia.org/food-assistance-given-to-canadian-residents-by-the-iglesia-ni-cristo/)through various socio-economic and humanitarian initiatives. Through acts of kindness, the INC continues its Scripture-inspired advocacy to “do good to those who need it” (Proverbs 3:27, Good News Translation).

At present, the INC has active members in 165 countries and territories. Its membership comprises 151 ethnicities and nationalities.

###

About the Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ)

For more about the Church and its activities, please visit [www.iglesianicristo.net](http://www.iglesianicristo.net/)or [https://incmedia.org/press-room/](https://incmedia.org/press-room/)

PHOTO

(none) Free packages at an INCGiving event at the Bob Snodgrass Recreation Complex in High River, Alberta. Free care packages filled with dry goods and other necessities PHOTO

INCGiving Volunteers and Guests INCGiving volunteer handing out free packages during an INCGiving event in High River, Alberta. Volunteer handing out free care packages at a community outreach event PHOTO

Guests Guest receiving free INCGiving packages in High River, Alberta. Group of people receiving free care packages

## Take a Weekly Adventure With Us!

Here you’ll get the chance to choose one episode to show a week to the INC Kids in your district. Feel free to also use them for any of your INC Kids activities!

## The Creation of All Things in the Beginning

In this episode of Bible Time, we learn how God created everything on earth – the birds, the trees, even the first man and woman!

## Dealing with Big Feelings

Sometimes, we may feel “big feelings”. But what are “big feelings”? And how can we keep them under control? Join Klay the Koala in this episode as he tells us what big feelings are and how to handle them!

## How to Do Better in School

Would you like to do well in school? Want to be a better student? In this episode of Klay the Koala, Klay gives us tips on how to do better in school.

![](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/02194817/klay-preview-coloring-sheets.png)

## INC Local Congregation of Riverside in California Receives 2024 Beautification Award

![A group of three individuals holds the Beautification Award while standing in front of the worship building, surrounded by landscaping and clean sidewalks.](https://2022incmediaorg.storage.googleapis.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/02/17001908/XL-PIO-OCC-001-JS-001-copy.jpg)Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ) Honored With “Keep Riverside Clean and Beautiful” Recognition

(RIVERSIDE, California) — The City of Riverside in California recognized the efforts of the Iglesia Ni Cristo (INC or Church Of Christ) to clean and beautify its surroundings, honoring the INC Local Congregation of Riverside as one of the recipients of the city’s “Beautification Award” for this year.

During the State of the City Address of Riverside Mayor Patricia Lock Dawson on January 25, a total of 17 awardees were honored across nine different categories. The event, attended by representatives of over 1,000 local businesses and organizations, was the culmination of the *Keep Riverside Clean & Beautiful*community program, which is sponsored by the City of Riverside’s Public Works Department and the Greater Riverside Chambers of Commerce. The mission of the program was to “instill a sense of community pride by creating partnerships that work toward the beautification of the City” as stated on the Award certificate.

One of the 17 awardees was the [Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ)](about:blank)Local Congregation of Riverside which was recognized for its exterior reconstruction, as well as the upkeep, general appearance and condition of its grounds on Locust Street.

Church Of Christ members strive to keep the INC houses of worship in excellent condition to bring honor to our Almighty God.

The INC Local Congregation of Riverside is one of 20 INC congregations throughout its [region](about:blank)in Southern California. The Iglesia Ni Cristo, with congregations in 165 countries and territories, remains a pillar in communities around the world. For more about the Church and its activities, please visit [www.iglesianicristo.net.](about:blank)

**Photos Courtesy of Iglesia Ni Cristo Public Information Office:**

The Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ) House of Worship of the Local Congregation of Riverside on Locust St in the City of Riverside, is recognized in the city’s 2024 Beautification Awards. Iglesia Ni Cristo Minister of the Gospel Brother Alfred Alexis Lorido accepting the Award during the Mayor’s State of the City Address. Iglesia Ni Cristo Minister of the Gospel Brother Alfred Alexis Lorido and Resident Worker of Riverside Brother Ron De Mesa hold the award given to the INC local congregation of Riverside California. With them are representatives of the City of Riverside. The 2024 Beautification Award Certificate awarded to the Iglesia Ni Cristo (Church Of Christ) Local Congregation of Riverside.

## INC Receives Special Recognitions in Guam and National City, California

In the month of July, special proclamations are made in Guam and National City declaring ‘Iglesia Ni Cristo’ Day and recognizing the Church Of Christ.
- [Church News](https://incmedia.org/tag/church-news/)

Pause Play 00:00 05:16 Unmute Mute Disable captions Enable captions Settings Captions Disabled Quality undefined Speed Normal Go back to previous menu Go back to previous menu Go back to previous menu 0.5× 0.75× Normal 1.25× 1.5× 1.75× 2× 4× PIP Exit fullscreen Enter fullscreen Play Pause Play 00:00 05:16 Unmute Mute Disable captions Enable captions Settings Captions Disabled Quality undefined Speed Normal Go back to previous menu Go back to previous menu Go back to previous menu 0.5× 0.75× Normal 1.25× 1.5× 1.75× 2× 4× PIP Exit fullscreen Enter fullscreen Play Play Higher ResolutionPlay Lower Resolution[Download Video](#elementor-action%3Aaction%3Dpopup%3Aopen%26settings%3DeyJpZCI6IjQ5MDMxIiwidG9nZ2xlIjpmYWxzZX0%3D)[Ask a Question](/contact/)Click to expand transcript

*[Show Open]*

**Brother Tyler Worthen:**As the Church Of Christ continues its celebrations of its 111th anniversary, we shift our attention to special proclamations from government officials that were made in recognition of the [church’s prolific](https://incmedia.org/inc-holds-baptisms-all-across-north-america/)contributions in communities in America. Let’s check it out.

**Gov. Lou Leon Guerrero:**I, Lou Leon Guerrero, Mega hagan Gahan (Governor of Guam) by virtue of the authority vested in me by the Organic Act of Guam as amended, do hereby proclaim July 27, 2025 in recognition of Iglesia Ni Cristo Day and celebration of the Church’s 111th anniversary.

**Brother Tyler Worthen:**Just recently, the governor of Guam, Lourdes, Leon Guerrero, presented the [Executive Minister, Brother Eduardo V. Manalo](https://incmedia.org/thank-you-brother-eduardo-v-manalo-for-the-inc-giving-project/)and the Church Of Christ a certificate of recognition for its 111th anniversary and its continuous outreach programs to the people of Guam. District Supervising Minister of Micronesia, Brother Joven Sepillo Jr, accepted on his and the Church’s behalf.

**Gov. Lou Leon Guerrero:**…and the influence of the Church and your work in the ministry of making sure that the people that you serve, making sure that you interact with the people to provide them with the services that are needed to continue on the [Christian values](https://incmedia.org/finding-good-advice-as-a-young-christian/)and to continue on like you said, the work of the Lord.

**Brother Tyler Worthen:**This work of extending care within communities, is a constant no matter where members are found, and it is evident here in Guam, where various outreach events through the years have been done aimed at helping their fellow men.

**Brother Joven Sepillo, Jr:**They are indeed recognizing the efforts of the brethren here in the district of Micronesia, particularly here in the island of Guam. It made us so joyful, and at the same time, it inspired us to all the more be dedicated, to be active in uniting with all the [projects being launched by the Church Administration](https://incmedia.org/what-can-this-church-offer-your-community/)to become good citizens of our country and to be good Christians.

**Brother Tyler Worthen:**Meanwhile in San Diego, California, National City Mayor Ron Morrison also officially proclaimed July 27 as Iglesia Ni Cristo or INC Day, acknowledging the Church’s global impact and its significant contributions to the community.

**Ron Morrison:**Now therefore be it resolved that I, Ron Morrison, Mayor, on behalf of the City Council, proclaim July 27, 2025 in the city of Nashville.

**Brother Tyler Worthen:**From City Hall, Mayor Ron Morrison officially declared July 27th as Iglesia Ni Cristo Day, as they also commemorate the Church’s 111th anniversary this year. [Church Of Christ members](https://incmedia.org/why-so-serious-about-church-membership/)gathered from around the ecclesiastical district of San Diego County to witness this momentous event.

**Speaker:**Their Church helps in giving aid to those in need and changing their quality of life, making it a better place to live.

**Brother Tyler Worthen:**Government officials attested to all the efforts of the [Church Of Christ](https://incmedia.org/10-things-about-the-iglesia-ni-cristo/)in continuing socio civic and outreach activities to the community.

**Mayor Ron Morrison:**The Iglesia Ni Cristo has grown into a global religious organization, uplifting individuals through spiritual guidance, community engagement and a steadfast commitment to unity, compassion and humanitarian services.

**Mayor Ron Morrison:**The example that you guys provide is awesome. Makes a huge difference.

**Brother Tyler Worthen:**Proclamations like these serve as further inspiration for Church Of Christ members in Guam, National City, and around the world to fulfill God’s work, as well as reaching out and caring for others, especially in times of need.

**Brother Tyler Worthen:**All praises be to the [Almighty God](https://incmedia.org/why-god-is-not-a-trinity/)as members continue to reflect on all of God’s great blessings, the recognitions the Church receives from public servants give inspiration for all to continue uniting in every Church endeavor.

**Brother Tyler Worthen:**Well, that does it for this episode of INC News World. Tune in every Sunday for new episodes on INC TV and every Wednesday on our YouTube channel and on streaming platforms. I’m Brother Tyler Worthen, thank you for joining us. Stay safe and we’ll see you next time. [God bless](https://incmedia.org/they-will-always-find-gods-blessing-and-grace/)you.

*[On-screen logo graphic]*

*INC NEW WORLD*

*INC MEDIA SERVICES*

*All Rights Reserved 2025*
